2032 }
2033
2034 if (!TargetScalarType)
2035 return false;
2036
2037 assert(!LI->user_empty() && "Unexpected load without bitcast users");
2038 InstructionCost ScalarizedCost =
2039 TTI.getMemoryOpCost(Instruction::Load, TargetScalarType, LI->getAlign(),
2040 LI->getPointerAddressSpace(), CostKind);
2041
2042 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found vector load feeding only bitcasts: " << *LI
2043 << "\n OriginalCost: " << OriginalCost
2044 << " vs ScalarizedCost: " << ScalarizedCost << "\n");
2045
2046 if (ScalarizedCost >= OriginalCost)
2047 return false;
2048
2049 // Ensure we add the load back to the worklist BEFORE its users so they can
2050 // erased in the correct order.
2051 Worklist.push(LI);
2052
2053 Builder.SetInsertPoint(LI);
2054 auto *ScalarLoad =
2055 Builder.CreateLoad(TargetScalarType, Ptr, LI->getName() + ".scalar");
2056 ScalarLoad->setAlignment(LI->getAlign());
2057 ScalarLoad->copyMetadata(*LI);
2058
2059 // Replace all bitcast users with the scalar load.
2060 for (User *U : LI->users()) {
2061 auto *BC = cast<BitCastInst>(U);
2062 replaceValue(*BC, *ScalarLoad, false);
2063 }
2064
2065 return true;
2066}
2067
2068bool VectorCombine::scalarizeExtExtract(Instruction &I) {
2069 if (!TTI.allowVectorElementIndexingUsingGEP())
2070 return false;
2071 auto *Ext = dyn_cast<ZExtInst>(&I);
2072 if (!Ext)
2073 return false;
2074
2075 // Try to convert a vector zext feeding only extracts to a set of scalar
2076 // (Src << ExtIdx *Size) & (Size -1)
2077 // if profitable .
2078 auto *SrcTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(Ext->getOperand(0)->getType());
2079 if (!SrcTy)
2080 return false;
2081 auto *DstTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(Ext->getType());
2082
2083 Type *ScalarDstTy = DstTy->getElementType();
2084 if (DL->getTypeSizeInBits(SrcTy) != DL->getTypeSizeInBits(ScalarDstTy))
2085 return false;
2086
2087 InstructionCost VectorCost =
2088 TTI.getCastInstrCost(Instruction::ZExt, DstTy, SrcTy,
2089 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind, Ext);
2090 unsigned ExtCnt = 0;
2091 bool ExtLane0 = false;
2092 for (User *U : Ext->users()) {
2093 uint64_t Idx;
2094 if (!match(U, m_ExtractElt(m_Value(), m_ConstantInt(Idx))))
2095 return false;
2096 if (cast<Instruction>(U)->use_empty())
2097 continue;
2098 ExtCnt += 1;
2099 ExtLane0 |= !Idx;
2100 VectorCost += TTI.getVectorInstrCost(Instruction::ExtractElement, DstTy,
2101 CostKind, Idx, U);
2102 }
2103
2104 InstructionCost ScalarCost =
2105 ExtCnt * TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(
2106 Instruction::And, ScalarDstTy, CostKind,
2107 {TTI::OK_AnyValue, TTI::OP_None},
2108 {TTI::OK_NonUniformConstantValue, TTI::OP_None}) +
2109 (ExtCnt - ExtLane0) *
2110 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(
2111 Instruction::LShr, ScalarDstTy, CostKind,
2112 {TTI::OK_AnyValue, TTI::OP_None},
2113 {TTI::OK_NonUniformConstantValue, TTI::OP_None});
2114 if (ScalarCost > VectorCost)
2115 return false;
2116
2117 Value *ScalarV = Ext->getOperand(0);
2118 if (!isGuaranteedNotToBePoison(ScalarV, &AC, dyn_cast<Instruction>(ScalarV),
2119 &DT)) {
2120 // Check wether all lanes are extracted, all extracts trigger UB
2121 // on poison, and the last extract (and hence all previous ones)
2122 // are guaranteed to execute if Ext executes. If so, we do not
2123 // need to insert a freeze.
2124 SmallDenseSet<ConstantInt *, 8> ExtractedLanes;
2125 bool AllExtractsTriggerUB = true;
2126 ExtractElementInst *LastExtract = nullptr;
2127 BasicBlock *ExtBB = Ext->getParent();
2128 for (User *U : Ext->users()) {
2129 auto *Extract = cast<ExtractElementInst>(U);
2130 if (Extract->getParent() != ExtBB || !programUndefinedIfPoison(Extract)) {
2131 AllExtractsTriggerUB = false;
2132 break;
2133 }
2134 ExtractedLanes.insert(cast<ConstantInt>(Extract->getIndexOperand()));
2135 if (!LastExtract || LastExtract->comesBefore(Extract))
2136 LastExtract = Extract;
2137 }
2138 if (ExtractedLanes.size() != DstTy->getNumElements() ||
2139 !AllExtractsTriggerUB ||
2140 !isGuaranteedToTransferExecutionToSuccessor(Ext->getIterator(),
2141 LastExtract->getIterator()))
2142 ScalarV = Builder.CreateFreeze(ScalarV);
2143 }
2144 ScalarV = Builder.CreateBitCast(
2145 ScalarV,
2146 IntegerType::get(SrcTy->getContext(), DL->getTypeSizeInBits(SrcTy)));
2147 uint64_t SrcEltSizeInBits = DL->getTypeSizeInBits(SrcTy->getElementType());
2148 uint64_t TotalBits = DL->getTypeSizeInBits(SrcTy);
2149 APInt EltBitMask = APInt::getLowBitsSet(TotalBits, SrcEltSizeInBits);
2150 Type *PackedTy = IntegerType::get(SrcTy->getContext(), TotalBits);
2151 Value *Mask = ConstantInt::get(PackedTy, EltBitMask);
2152 for (User *U : Ext->users()) {
2153 auto *Extract = cast<ExtractElementInst>(U);
2154 uint64_t Idx =
2155 cast<ConstantInt>(Extract->getIndexOperand())->getZExtValue();
2156 uint64_t ShiftAmt =
2157 DL->isBigEndian()
2158 ? (TotalBits - SrcEltSizeInBits - Idx * SrcEltSizeInBits)
2159 : (Idx * SrcEltSizeInBits);
2160 Value *LShr = Builder.CreateLShr(ScalarV, ShiftAmt);
2161 Value *And = Builder.CreateAnd(LShr, Mask);
2162 U->replaceAllUsesWith(And);
2163 }
2164 return true;
2165}
2166
2167/// Try to fold "(or (zext (bitcast X)), (shl (zext (bitcast Y)), C))"
2168/// to "(bitcast (concat X, Y))"
2169/// where X/Y are bitcasted from i1 mask vectors.
2170bool VectorCombine::foldConcatOfBoolMasks(Instruction &I) {
2171 Type *Ty = I.getType();
2172 if (!Ty->isIntegerTy())
2173 return false;
2174
2175 // TODO: Add big endian test coverage
2176 if (DL->isBigEndian())
2177 return false;
2178
2179 // Restrict to disjoint cases so the mask vectors aren't overlapping.
2180 Instruction *X, *Y;
2181 if (!match(&I, m_DisjointOr(m_Instruction(X), m_Instruction(Y))))
2182 return false;
2183
2184 // Allow both sources to contain shl, to handle more generic pattern:
2185 // "(or (shl (zext (bitcast X)), C1), (shl (zext (bitcast Y)), C2))"
2186 Value *SrcX;
2187 uint64_t ShAmtX = 0;
2188 if (!match(X, m_OneUse(m_ZExt(m_OneUse(m_BitCast(m_Value(SrcX)))))) &&
2189 !match(X, m_OneUse(
2190 m_Shl(m_OneUse(m_ZExt(m_OneUse(m_BitCast(m_Value(SrcX))))),
2191 m_ConstantInt(ShAmtX)))))
2192 return false;
2193
2194 Value *SrcY;
2195 uint64_t ShAmtY = 0;
2196 if (!match(Y, m_OneUse(m_ZExt(m_OneUse(m_BitCast(m_Value(SrcY)))))) &&
2197 !match(Y, m_OneUse(
2198 m_Shl(m_OneUse(m_ZExt(m_OneUse(m_BitCast(m_Value(SrcY))))),
2199 m_ConstantInt(ShAmtY)))))
2200 return false;
2201
2202 // Canonicalize larger shift to the RHS.
2203 if (ShAmtX > ShAmtY) {
2204 std::swap(X, Y);
2205 std::swap(SrcX, SrcY);
2206 std::swap(ShAmtX, ShAmtY);
2207 }
2208
2209 // Ensure both sources are matching vXi1 bool mask types, and that the shift
2210 // difference is the mask width so they can be easily concatenated together.
2211 uint64_t ShAmtDiff = ShAmtY - ShAmtX;
2212 unsigned NumSHL = (ShAmtX > 0) + (ShAmtY > 0);
2213 unsigned BitWidth = Ty->getPrimitiveSizeInBits();
2214 auto *MaskTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(SrcX->getType());
2215 if (!MaskTy || SrcX->getType() != SrcY->getType() ||
2216 !MaskTy->getElementType()->isIntegerTy(1) ||
2217 MaskTy->getNumElements() != ShAmtDiff ||
2218 MaskTy->getNumElements() > (BitWidth / 2))
2219 return false;
2220
2221 auto *ConcatTy = FixedVectorType::getDoubleElementsVectorType(MaskTy);
2222 auto *ConcatIntTy =
2223 Type::getIntNTy(Ty->getContext(), ConcatTy->getNumElements());
2224 auto *MaskIntTy = Type::getIntNTy(Ty->getContext(), ShAmtDiff);
2225
2226 SmallVector<int, 32> ConcatMask(ConcatTy->getNumElements());
2227 std::iota(ConcatMask.begin(), ConcatMask.end(), 0);
2228
2229 // TODO: Is it worth supporting multi use cases?
2230 InstructionCost OldCost = 0;
2231 OldCost += TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Instruction::Or, Ty, CostKind);
2232 OldCost +=
2233 NumSHL * TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Instruction::Shl, Ty, CostKind);
2234 OldCost += 2 * TTI.getCastInstrCost(Instruction::ZExt, Ty, MaskIntTy,
2235 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2236 OldCost += 2 * TTI.getCastInstrCost(Instruction::BitCast, MaskIntTy, MaskTy,
2237 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2238
2239 InstructionCost NewCost = 0;
2240 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, ConcatTy,
2241 MaskTy, ConcatMask, CostKind);
2242 NewCost += TTI.getCastInstrCost(Instruction::BitCast, ConcatIntTy, ConcatTy,
2243 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2244 if (Ty != ConcatIntTy)
2245 NewCost += TTI.getCastInstrCost(Instruction::ZExt, Ty, ConcatIntTy,
2246 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2247 if (ShAmtX > 0)
2248 NewCost += TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Instruction::Shl, Ty, CostKind);
2249
2250 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a concatenation of bitcasted bool masks: " << I
2251 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
2252 << "\n");
2253
2254 if (NewCost > OldCost)
2255 return false;
2256
2257 // Build bool mask concatenation, bitcast back to scalar integer, and perform
2258 // any residual zero-extension or shifting.
2259 Value *Concat = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(SrcX, SrcY, ConcatMask);
2260 Worklist.pushValue(Concat);
2261
2262 Value *Result = Builder.CreateBitCast(Concat, ConcatIntTy);
2263
2264 if (Ty != ConcatIntTy) {
2265 Worklist.pushValue(Result);
2266 Result = Builder.CreateZExt(Result, Ty);
2267 }
2268
2269 if (ShAmtX > 0) {
2270 Worklist.pushValue(Result);
2271 Result = Builder.CreateShl(Result, ShAmtX);
2272 }
2273
2274 replaceValue(I, *Result);
2275 return true;
2276}
2277
2278/// Try to convert "shuffle (binop (shuffle, shuffle)), undef"
2279/// --> "binop (shuffle), (shuffle)".
2280bool VectorCombine::foldPermuteOfBinops(Instruction &I) {
2281 BinaryOperator *BinOp;
2282 ArrayRef<int> OuterMask;
2283 if (!match(&I, m_Shuffle(m_BinOp(BinOp), m_Undef(), m_Mask(OuterMask))))
2284 return false;
2285
2286 // Don't introduce poison into div/rem.
2287 if (BinOp->isIntDivRem() && llvm::is_contained(OuterMask, PoisonMaskElem))
2288 return false;
2289
2290 Value *Op00, *Op01, *Op10, *Op11;
2291 ArrayRef<int> Mask0, Mask1;
2292 bool Match0 = match(BinOp->getOperand(0),
2293 m_Shuffle(m_Value(Op00), m_Value(Op01), m_Mask(Mask0)));
2294 bool Match1 = match(BinOp->getOperand(1),
2295 m_Shuffle(m_Value(Op10), m_Value(Op11), m_Mask(Mask1)));
2296 if (!Match0 && !Match1)
2297 return false;
2298
2299 Op00 = Match0 ? Op00 : BinOp->getOperand(0);
2300 Op01 = Match0 ? Op01 : BinOp->getOperand(0);
2301 Op10 = Match1 ? Op10 : BinOp->getOperand(1);
2302 Op11 = Match1 ? Op11 : BinOp->getOperand(1);
2303
2304 Instruction::BinaryOps Opcode = BinOp->getOpcode();
2305 auto *ShuffleDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
2306 auto *BinOpTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(BinOp->getType());
2307 auto *Op0Ty = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(Op00->getType());
2308 auto *Op1Ty = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(Op10->getType());
2309 if (!ShuffleDstTy || !BinOpTy || !Op0Ty || !Op1Ty)
2310 return false;
2311
2312 unsigned NumSrcElts = BinOpTy->getNumElements();
2313
2314 // Don't accept shuffles that reference the second operand in
2315 // div/rem or if its an undef arg.
2316 if ((BinOp->isIntDivRem() || !isa<PoisonValue>(I.getOperand(1))) &&
2317 any_of(OuterMask, [NumSrcElts](int M) { return M >= (int)NumSrcElts; }))
2318 return false;
2319
2320 // Merge outer / inner (or identity if no match) shuffles.
2321 SmallVector<int> NewMask0, NewMask1;
2322 for (int M : OuterMask) {
2323 if (M < 0 || M >= (int)NumSrcElts) {
2324 NewMask0.push_back(PoisonMaskElem);
2325 NewMask1.push_back(PoisonMaskElem);
2326 } else {
2327 NewMask0.push_back(Match0 ? Mask0[M] : M);
2328 NewMask1.push_back(Match1 ? Mask1[M] : M);
2329 }
2330 }
2331
2332 unsigned NumOpElts = Op0Ty->getNumElements();
2333 bool IsIdentity0 = ShuffleDstTy == Op0Ty &&
2334 all_of(NewMask0, [NumOpElts](int M) { return M < (int)NumOpElts; }) &&
2335 ShuffleVectorInst::isIdentityMask(NewMask0, NumOpElts);
2336 bool IsIdentity1 = ShuffleDstTy == Op1Ty &&
2337 all_of(NewMask1, [NumOpElts](int M) { return M < (int)NumOpElts; }) &&
2338 ShuffleVectorInst::isIdentityMask(NewMask1, NumOpElts);
2339
2340 InstructionCost NewCost = 0;
2341 // Try to merge shuffles across the binop if the new shuffles are not costly.
2342 InstructionCost BinOpCost =
2343 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Opcode, BinOpTy, CostKind);
2344 InstructionCost OldCost =
2345 BinOpCost + TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc,
2346 ShuffleDstTy, BinOpTy, OuterMask, CostKind,
2347 0, nullptr, {BinOp}, &I);
2348 if (!BinOp->hasOneUse())
2349 NewCost += BinOpCost;
2350
2351 if (Match0) {
2352 InstructionCost Shuf0Cost = TTI.getShuffleCost(
2353 TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, BinOpTy, Op0Ty, Mask0, CostKind,
2354 0, nullptr, {Op00, Op01}, cast<Instruction>(BinOp->getOperand(0)));
2355 OldCost += Shuf0Cost;
2356 if (!BinOp->hasOneUse() || !BinOp->getOperand(0)->hasOneUse())
2357 NewCost += Shuf0Cost;
2358 }
2359 if (Match1) {
2360 InstructionCost Shuf1Cost = TTI.getShuffleCost(
2361 TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, BinOpTy, Op0Ty, Mask0, CostKind,
2362 0, nullptr, {Op10, Op11}, cast<Instruction>(BinOp->getOperand(1)));
2363 OldCost += Shuf1Cost;
2364 if (!BinOp->hasOneUse() || !BinOp->getOperand(1)->hasOneUse())
2365 NewCost += Shuf1Cost;
2366 }
2367
2368 NewCost += TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Opcode, ShuffleDstTy, CostKind);
2369
2370 if (!IsIdentity0)
2371 NewCost +=
2372 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, ShuffleDstTy,
2373 Op0Ty, NewMask0, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {Op00, Op01});
2374 if (!IsIdentity1)
2375 NewCost +=
2376 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, ShuffleDstTy,
2377 Op1Ty, NewMask1, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {Op10, Op11});
2378
2379 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a shuffle feeding a shuffled binop: " << I
2380 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
2381 << "\n");
2382
2383 // If costs are equal, still fold as we reduce instruction count.
2384 if (NewCost > OldCost)
2385 return false;
2386
2387 Value *LHS =
2388 IsIdentity0 ? Op00 : Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Op00, Op01, NewMask0);
2389 Value *RHS =
2390 IsIdentity1 ? Op10 : Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Op10, Op11, NewMask1);
2391 Value *NewBO = Builder.CreateBinOp(Opcode, LHS, RHS);
2392
2393 // Intersect flags from the old binops.
2394 if (auto *NewInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(NewBO))
2395 NewInst->copyIRFlags(BinOp);
2396
2397 Worklist.pushValue(LHS);
2398 Worklist.pushValue(RHS);
2399 replaceValue(I, *NewBO);
2400 return true;
2401}
2402
2403/// Try to convert "shuffle (binop), (binop)" into "binop (shuffle), (shuffle)".
2404/// Try to convert "shuffle (cmpop), (cmpop)" into "cmpop (shuffle), (shuffle)".
2405bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleOfBinops(Instruction &I) {
2406 ArrayRef<int> OldMask;
2407 Instruction *LHS, *RHS;
2408 if (!match(&I, m_Shuffle(m_OneUse(m_Instruction(LHS)),
2409 m_OneUse(m_Instruction(RHS)), m_Mask(OldMask))))
2410 return false;
2411
2412 // TODO: Add support for addlike etc.
2413 if (LHS->getOpcode() != RHS->getOpcode())
2414 return false;
2415
2416 Value *X, *Y, *Z, *W;
2417 bool IsCommutative = false;
2418 CmpPredicate PredLHS = CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE;
2419 CmpPredicate PredRHS = CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE;
2420 if (match(LHS, m_BinOp(m_Value(X), m_Value(Y))) &&
2421 match(RHS, m_BinOp(m_Value(Z), m_Value(W)))) {
2422 auto *BO = cast<BinaryOperator>(LHS);
2423 // Don't introduce poison into div/rem.
2424 if (llvm::is_contained(OldMask, PoisonMaskElem) && BO->isIntDivRem())
2425 return false;
2426 IsCommutative = BinaryOperator::isCommutative(BO->getOpcode());
2427 } else if (match(LHS, m_Cmp(PredLHS, m_Value(X), m_Value(Y))) &&
2428 match(RHS, m_Cmp(PredRHS, m_Value(Z), m_Value(W))) &&
2429 (CmpInst::Predicate)PredLHS == (CmpInst::Predicate)PredRHS) {
2430 IsCommutative = cast<CmpInst>(LHS)->isCommutative();
2431 } else
2432 return false;
2433
2434 auto *ShuffleDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
2435 auto *BinResTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(LHS->getType());
2436 auto *BinOpTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(X->getType());
2437 if (!ShuffleDstTy || !BinResTy || !BinOpTy || X->getType() != Z->getType())
2438 return false;
2439
2440 unsigned NumSrcElts = BinOpTy->getNumElements();
2441
2442 // If we have something like "add X, Y" and "add Z, X", swap ops to match.
2443 if (IsCommutative && X != Z && Y != W && (X == W || Y == Z))
2444 std::swap(X, Y);
2445
2446 auto ConvertToUnary = [NumSrcElts](int &M) {
2447 if (M >= (int)NumSrcElts)
2448 M -= NumSrcElts;
2449 };
2450
2451 SmallVector<int> NewMask0(OldMask);
2452 TargetTransformInfo::ShuffleKind SK0 = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc;
2453 if (X == Z) {
2454 llvm::for_each(NewMask0, ConvertToUnary);
2455 SK0 = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc;
2456 Z = PoisonValue::get(BinOpTy);
2457 }
2458
2459 SmallVector<int> NewMask1(OldMask);
2460 TargetTransformInfo::ShuffleKind SK1 = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc;
2461 if (Y == W) {
2462 llvm::for_each(NewMask1, ConvertToUnary);
2463 SK1 = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc;
2464 W = PoisonValue::get(BinOpTy);
2465 }
2466
2467 // Try to replace a binop with a shuffle if the shuffle is not costly.
2468 InstructionCost OldCost =
2469 TTI.getInstructionCost(LHS, CostKind) +
2470 TTI.getInstructionCost(RHS, CostKind) +
2471 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, ShuffleDstTy,
2472 BinResTy, OldMask, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {LHS, RHS},
2473 &I);
2474
2475 // Handle shuffle(binop(shuffle(x),y),binop(z,shuffle(w))) style patterns
2476 // where one use shuffles have gotten split across the binop/cmp. These
2477 // often allow a major reduction in total cost that wouldn't happen as
2478 // individual folds.
2479 auto MergeInner = [&](Value *&Op, int Offset, MutableArrayRef<int> Mask,
2480 TTI::TargetCostKind CostKind) -> bool {
2481 Value *InnerOp;
2482 ArrayRef<int> InnerMask;
2483 if (match(Op, m_OneUse(m_Shuffle(m_Value(InnerOp), m_Undef(),
2484 m_Mask(InnerMask)))) &&
2485 InnerOp->getType() == Op->getType() &&
2486 all_of(InnerMask,
2487 [NumSrcElts](int M) { return M < (int)NumSrcElts; })) {
2488 for (int &M : Mask)
2489 if (Offset <= M && M < (int)(Offset + NumSrcElts)) {
2490 M = InnerMask[M - Offset];
2491 M = 0 <= M ? M + Offset : M;
2492 }
2493 OldCost += TTI.getInstructionCost(cast<Instruction>(Op), CostKind);
2494 Op = InnerOp;
2495 return true;
2496 }
2497 return false;
2498 };
2499 bool ReducedInstCount = false;
2500 ReducedInstCount |= MergeInner(X, 0, NewMask0, CostKind);
2501 ReducedInstCount |= MergeInner(Y, 0, NewMask1, CostKind);
2502 ReducedInstCount |= MergeInner(Z, NumSrcElts, NewMask0, CostKind);
2503 ReducedInstCount |= MergeInner(W, NumSrcElts, NewMask1, CostKind);
2504 bool SingleSrcBinOp = (X == Y) && (Z == W) && (NewMask0 == NewMask1);
2505 ReducedInstCount |= SingleSrcBinOp;
2506
2507 auto *ShuffleCmpTy =
2508 FixedVectorType::get(BinOpTy->getElementType(), ShuffleDstTy);
2509 InstructionCost NewCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(
2510 SK0, ShuffleCmpTy, BinOpTy, NewMask0, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {X, Z});
2511 if (!SingleSrcBinOp)
2512 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(SK1, ShuffleCmpTy, BinOpTy, NewMask1,
2513 CostKind, 0, nullptr, {Y, W});
2514
2515 if (PredLHS == CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE) {
2516 NewCost +=
2517 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(LHS->getOpcode(), ShuffleDstTy, CostKind);
2518 } else {
2519 NewCost += TTI.getCmpSelInstrCost(LHS->getOpcode(), ShuffleCmpTy,
2520 ShuffleDstTy, PredLHS, CostKind);
2521 }
2522
2523 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a shuffle feeding two binops: " << I
2524 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
2525 << "\n");
2526
2527 // If either shuffle will constant fold away, then fold for the same cost as
2528 // we will reduce the instruction count.
2529 ReducedInstCount |= (isa<Constant>(X) && isa<Constant>(Z)) ||
2530 (isa<Constant>(Y) && isa<Constant>(W));
2531 if (ReducedInstCount ? (NewCost > OldCost) : (NewCost >= OldCost))
2532 return false;
2533
2534 Value *Shuf0 = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(X, Z, NewMask0);
2535 Value *Shuf1 =
2536 SingleSrcBinOp ? Shuf0 : Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Y, W, NewMask1);
2537 Value *NewBO = PredLHS == CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE
2538 ? Builder.CreateBinOp(
2539 cast<BinaryOperator>(LHS)->getOpcode(), Shuf0, Shuf1)
2540 : Builder.CreateCmp(PredLHS, Shuf0, Shuf1);
2541
2542 // Intersect flags from the old binops.
2543 if (auto *NewInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(NewBO)) {
2544 NewInst->copyIRFlags(LHS);
2545 NewInst->andIRFlags(RHS);
2546 }
2547
2548 Worklist.pushValue(Shuf0);
2549 Worklist.pushValue(Shuf1);
2550 replaceValue(I, *NewBO);
2551 return true;
2552}
2553
2554/// Try to convert,
2555/// (shuffle(select(c1,t1,f1)), (select(c2,t2,f2)), m) into
2556/// (select (shuffle c1,c2,m), (shuffle t1,t2,m), (shuffle f1,f2,m))
2557bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleOfSelects(Instruction &I) {
2558 ArrayRef<int> Mask;
2559 Value *C1, *T1, *F1, *C2, *T2, *F2;
2560 if (!match(&I, m_Shuffle(m_Select(m_Value(C1), m_Value(T1), m_Value(F1)),
2561 m_Select(m_Value(C2), m_Value(T2), m_Value(F2)),
2562 m_Mask(Mask))))
2563 return false;
2564
2565 auto *Sel1 = cast<Instruction>(I.getOperand(0));
2566 auto *Sel2 = cast<Instruction>(I.getOperand(1));
2567
2568 auto *C1VecTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(C1->getType());
2569 auto *C2VecTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(C2->getType());
2570 if (!C1VecTy || !C2VecTy || C1VecTy != C2VecTy)
2571 return false;
2572
2573 auto *SI0FOp = dyn_cast<FPMathOperator>(I.getOperand(0));
2574 auto *SI1FOp = dyn_cast<FPMathOperator>(I.getOperand(1));
2575 // SelectInsts must have the same FMF.
2576 if (((SI0FOp == nullptr) != (SI1FOp == nullptr)) ||
2577 ((SI0FOp != nullptr) &&
2578 (SI0FOp->getFastMathFlags() != SI1FOp->getFastMathFlags())))
2579 return false;
2580
2581 auto *SrcVecTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(T1->getType());
2582 auto *DstVecTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
2583 auto SK = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc;
2584 auto SelOp = Instruction::Select;
2585
2586 InstructionCost CostSel1 = TTI.getCmpSelInstrCost(
2587 SelOp, SrcVecTy, C1VecTy, CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE, CostKind);
2588 InstructionCost CostSel2 = TTI.getCmpSelInstrCost(
2589 SelOp, SrcVecTy, C2VecTy, CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE, CostKind);
2590
2591 InstructionCost OldCost =
2592 CostSel1 + CostSel2 +
2593 TTI.getShuffleCost(SK, DstVecTy, SrcVecTy, Mask, CostKind, 0, nullptr,
2594 {I.getOperand(0), I.getOperand(1)}, &I);
2595
2596 InstructionCost NewCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(
2597 SK, FixedVectorType::get(C1VecTy->getScalarType(), Mask.size()), C1VecTy,
2598 Mask, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {C1, C2});
2599 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(SK, DstVecTy, SrcVecTy, Mask, CostKind, 0,
2600 nullptr, {T1, T2});
2601 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(SK, DstVecTy, SrcVecTy, Mask, CostKind, 0,
2602 nullptr, {F1, F2});
2603 auto *C1C2ShuffledVecTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(
2604 toVectorTy(Type::getInt1Ty(I.getContext()), DstVecTy->getNumElements()));
2605 NewCost += TTI.getCmpSelInstrCost(SelOp, DstVecTy, C1C2ShuffledVecTy,
2606 CmpInst::BAD_ICMP_PREDICATE, CostKind);
2607
2608 if (!Sel1->hasOneUse())
2609 NewCost += CostSel1;
2610 if (!Sel2->hasOneUse())
2611 NewCost += CostSel2;
2612
2613 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a shuffle feeding two selects: " << I
2614 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
2615 << "\n");
2616 if (NewCost > OldCost)
2617 return false;
2618
2619 Value *ShuffleCmp = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(C1, C2, Mask);
2620 Value *ShuffleTrue = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(T1, T2, Mask);
2621 Value *ShuffleFalse = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(F1, F2, Mask);
2622 Value *NewSel;
2623 // We presuppose that the SelectInsts have the same FMF.
2624 if (SI0FOp)
2625 NewSel = Builder.CreateSelectFMF(ShuffleCmp, ShuffleTrue, ShuffleFalse,
2626 SI0FOp->getFastMathFlags());
2627 else
2628 NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(ShuffleCmp, ShuffleTrue, ShuffleFalse);
2629
2630 Worklist.pushValue(ShuffleCmp);
2631 Worklist.pushValue(ShuffleTrue);
2632 Worklist.pushValue(ShuffleFalse);
2633 replaceValue(I, *NewSel);
2634 return true;
2635}
2636
2637/// Try to convert "shuffle (castop), (castop)" with a shared castop operand
2638/// into "castop (shuffle)".
2639bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleOfCastops(Instruction &I) {
2640 Value *V0, *V1;
2641 ArrayRef<int> OldMask;
2642 if (!match(&I, m_Shuffle(m_Value(V0), m_Value(V1), m_Mask(OldMask))))
2643 return false;
2644
2645 // Check whether this is a binary shuffle.
2646 bool IsBinaryShuffle = !isa<UndefValue>(V1);
2647
2648 auto *C0 = dyn_cast<CastInst>(V0);
2649 auto *C1 = dyn_cast<CastInst>(V1);
2650 if (!C0 || (IsBinaryShuffle && !C1))
2651 return false;
2652
2653 Instruction::CastOps Opcode = C0->getOpcode();
2654
2655 // If this is allowed, foldShuffleOfCastops can get stuck in a loop
2656 // with foldBitcastOfShuffle. Reject in favor of foldBitcastOfShuffle.
2657 if (!IsBinaryShuffle && Opcode == Instruction::BitCast)
2658 return false;
2659
2660 if (IsBinaryShuffle) {
2661 if (C0->getSrcTy() != C1->getSrcTy())
2662 return false;
2663 // Handle shuffle(zext_nneg(x), sext(y)) -> sext(shuffle(x,y)) folds.
2664 if (Opcode != C1->getOpcode()) {
2665 if (match(C0, m_SExtLike(m_Value())) && match(C1, m_SExtLike(m_Value())))
2666 Opcode = Instruction::SExt;
2667 else
2668 return false;
2669 }
2670 }
2671
2672 auto *ShuffleDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
2673 auto *CastDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(C0->getDestTy());
2674 auto *CastSrcTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(C0->getSrcTy());
2675 if (!ShuffleDstTy || !CastDstTy || !CastSrcTy)
2676 return false;
2677
2678 unsigned NumSrcElts = CastSrcTy->getNumElements();
2679 unsigned NumDstElts = CastDstTy->getNumElements();
2680 assert((NumDstElts == NumSrcElts || Opcode == Instruction::BitCast) &&
2681 "Only bitcasts expected to alter src/dst element counts");
2682
2683 // Check for bitcasting of unscalable vector types.
2684 // e.g. <32 x i40> -> <40 x i32>
2685 if (NumDstElts != NumSrcElts && (NumSrcElts % NumDstElts) != 0 &&
2686 (NumDstElts % NumSrcElts) != 0)
2687 return false;
2688
2689 SmallVector<int, 16> NewMask;
2690 if (NumSrcElts >= NumDstElts) {
2691 // The bitcast is from wide to narrow/equal elements. The shuffle mask can
2692 // always be expanded to the equivalent form choosing narrower elements.
2693 assert(NumSrcElts % NumDstElts == 0 && "Unexpected shuffle mask");
2694 unsigned ScaleFactor = NumSrcElts / NumDstElts;
2695 narrowShuffleMaskElts(ScaleFactor, OldMask, NewMask);
2696 } else {
2697 // The bitcast is from narrow elements to wide elements. The shuffle mask
2698 // must choose consecutive elements to allow casting first.
2699 assert(NumDstElts % NumSrcElts == 0 && "Unexpected shuffle mask");
2700 unsigned ScaleFactor = NumDstElts / NumSrcElts;
2701 if (!widenShuffleMaskElts(ScaleFactor, OldMask, NewMask))
2702 return false;
2703 }
2704
2705 auto *NewShuffleDstTy =
2706 FixedVectorType::get(CastSrcTy->getScalarType(), NewMask.size());
2707
2708 // Try to replace a castop with a shuffle if the shuffle is not costly.
2709 InstructionCost CostC0 =
2710 TTI.getCastInstrCost(C0->getOpcode(), CastDstTy, CastSrcTy,
2711 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2712
2713 TargetTransformInfo::ShuffleKind ShuffleKind;
2714 if (IsBinaryShuffle)
2715 ShuffleKind = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc;
2716 else
2717 ShuffleKind = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc;
2718
2719 InstructionCost OldCost = CostC0;
2720 OldCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(ShuffleKind, ShuffleDstTy, CastDstTy, OldMask,
2721 CostKind, 0, nullptr, {}, &I);
2722
2723 InstructionCost NewCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(ShuffleKind, NewShuffleDstTy,
2724 CastSrcTy, NewMask, CostKind);
2725 NewCost += TTI.getCastInstrCost(Opcode, ShuffleDstTy, NewShuffleDstTy,
2726 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2727 if (!C0->hasOneUse())
2728 NewCost += CostC0;
2729 if (IsBinaryShuffle) {
2730 InstructionCost CostC1 =
2731 TTI.getCastInstrCost(C1->getOpcode(), CastDstTy, CastSrcTy,
2732 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
2733 OldCost += CostC1;
2734 if (!C1->hasOneUse())
2735 NewCost += CostC1;
2736 }
2737
2738 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a shuffle feeding two casts: " << I
2739 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
2740 << "\n");
2741 if (NewCost > OldCost)
2742 return false;
2743
2744 Value *Shuf;
2745 if (IsBinaryShuffle)
2746 Shuf = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(C0->getOperand(0), C1->getOperand(0),
2747 NewMask);
2748 else
2749 Shuf = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(C0->getOperand(0), NewMask);
2750
2751 Value *Cast = Builder.CreateCast(Opcode, Shuf, ShuffleDstTy);
2752
2753 // Intersect flags from the old casts.
2754 if (auto *NewInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(Cast)) {
2755 NewInst->copyIRFlags(C0);
2756 if (IsBinaryShuffle)
2757 NewInst->andIRFlags(C1);
2758 }
2759
2760 Worklist.pushValue(Shuf);
2761 replaceValue(I, *Cast);
2762 return true;
2763}
2764
2765/// Try to convert any of:
2766/// "shuffle (shuffle x, y), (shuffle y, x)"
2767/// "shuffle (shuffle x, undef), (shuffle y, undef)"
2768/// "shuffle (shuffle x, undef), y"
2769/// "shuffle x, (shuffle y, undef)"
2770/// into "shuffle x, y".
2771bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleOfShuffles(Instruction &I) {
2772 ArrayRef<int> OuterMask;
2773 Value *OuterV0, *OuterV1;
2774 if (!match(&I,
2775 m_Shuffle(m_Value(OuterV0), m_Value(OuterV1), m_Mask(OuterMask))))
2776 return false;
2777
2778 ArrayRef<int> InnerMask0, InnerMask1;
2779 Value *X0, *X1, *Y0, *Y1;
2780 bool Match0 =
2781 match(OuterV0, m_Shuffle(m_Value(X0), m_Value(Y0), m_Mask(InnerMask0)));
2782 bool Match1 =
2783 match(OuterV1, m_Shuffle(m_Value(X1), m_Value(Y1), m_Mask(InnerMask1)));
2784 if (!Match0 && !Match1)
2785 return false;
2786
2787 // If the outer shuffle is a permute, then create a fake inner all-poison
2788 // shuffle. This is easier than accounting for length-changing shuffles below.
2789 SmallVector<int, 16> PoisonMask1;
2790 if (!Match1 && isa<PoisonValue>(OuterV1)) {
2791 X1 = X0;
2792 Y1 = Y0;
2793 PoisonMask1.append(InnerMask0.size(), PoisonMaskElem);
2794 InnerMask1 = PoisonMask1;
2795 Match1 = true; // fake match
2796 }
2797
2798 X0 = Match0 ? X0 : OuterV0;
2799 Y0 = Match0 ? Y0 : OuterV0;
2800 X1 = Match1 ? X1 : OuterV1;
2801 Y1 = Match1 ? Y1 : OuterV1;
2802 auto *ShuffleDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
2803 auto *ShuffleSrcTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(X0->getType());
2804 auto *ShuffleImmTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(OuterV0->getType());
2805 if (!ShuffleDstTy || !ShuffleSrcTy || !ShuffleImmTy ||
2806 X0->getType() != X1->getType())
2807 return false;
2808
2809 unsigned NumSrcElts = ShuffleSrcTy->getNumElements();
2810 unsigned NumImmElts = ShuffleImmTy->getNumElements();
2811
2812 // Attempt to merge shuffles, matching upto 2 source operands.
2813 // Replace index to a poison arg with PoisonMaskElem.
2814 // Bail if either inner masks reference an undef arg.
2815 SmallVector<int, 16> NewMask(OuterMask);
2816 Value *NewX = nullptr, *NewY = nullptr;
2817 for (int &M : NewMask) {
2818 Value *Src = nullptr;
2819 if (0 <= M && M < (int)NumImmElts) {
2820 Src = OuterV0;
2821 if (Match0) {
2822 M = InnerMask0[M];
2823 Src = M >= (int)NumSrcElts ? Y0 : X0;
2824 M = M >= (int)NumSrcElts ? (M - NumSrcElts) : M;
2825 }
2826 } else if (M >= (int)NumImmElts) {
2827 Src = OuterV1;
2828 M -= NumImmElts;
2829 if (Match1) {
2830 M = InnerMask1[M];
2831 Src = M >= (int)NumSrcElts ? Y1 : X1;
2832 M = M >= (int)NumSrcElts ? (M - NumSrcElts) : M;
2833 }
2834 }
2835 if (Src && M != PoisonMaskElem) {
2836 assert(0 <= M && M < (int)NumSrcElts && "Unexpected shuffle mask index");
2837 if (isa<UndefValue>(Src)) {
2838 // We've referenced an undef element - if its poison, update the shuffle
2839 // mask, else bail.
2840 if (!isa<PoisonValue>(Src))
2841 return false;
2842 M = PoisonMaskElem;
2843 continue;
2844 }
2845 if (!NewX || NewX == Src) {
2846 NewX = Src;
2847 continue;
2848 }
2849 if (!NewY || NewY == Src) {
2850 M += NumSrcElts;
2851 NewY = Src;
2852 continue;
2853 }
2854 return false;
2855 }
2856 }
2857
2858 if (!NewX)
2859 return PoisonValue::get(ShuffleDstTy);
2860 if (!NewY)
2861 NewY = PoisonValue::get(ShuffleSrcTy);
2862
2863 // Have we folded to an Identity shuffle?
2864 if (ShuffleVectorInst::isIdentityMask(NewMask, NumSrcElts)) {
2865 replaceValue(I, *NewX);
2866 return true;
2867 }
2868
2869 // Try to merge the shuffles if the new shuffle is not costly.
2870 InstructionCost InnerCost0 = 0;
2871 if (Match0)
2872 InnerCost0 = TTI.getInstructionCost(cast<User>(OuterV0), CostKind);
2873
2874 InstructionCost InnerCost1 = 0;
2875 if (Match1)
2876 InnerCost1 = TTI.getInstructionCost(cast<User>(OuterV1), CostKind);
2877
2878 InstructionCost OuterCost = TTI.getInstructionCost(&I, CostKind);
2879
2880 InstructionCost OldCost = InnerCost0 + InnerCost1 + OuterCost;
2881
2882 bool IsUnary = all_of(NewMask, [&](int M) { return M < (int)NumSrcElts; });
2883 TargetTransformInfo::ShuffleKind SK =
2884 IsUnary ? TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc
2885 : TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc;
2886 InstructionCost NewCost =
2887 TTI.getShuffleCost(SK, ShuffleDstTy, ShuffleSrcTy, NewMask, CostKind, 0,
2888 nullptr, {NewX, NewY});
2889 if (!OuterV0->hasOneUse())
2890 NewCost += InnerCost0;
2891 if (!OuterV1->hasOneUse())
2892 NewCost += InnerCost1;
2893
2894 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a shuffle feeding two shuffles: " << I
2895 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
2896 << "\n");
2897 if (NewCost > OldCost)
2898 return false;
2899
2900 Value *Shuf = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(NewX, NewY, NewMask);
2901 replaceValue(I, *Shuf);
2902 return true;
2903}
2904
2905/// Try to convert a chain of length-preserving shuffles that are fed by
2906/// length-changing shuffles from the same source, e.g. a chain of length 3:
2907///
2908/// "shuffle (shuffle (shuffle x, (shuffle y, undef)),
2909/// (shuffle y, undef)),
2910// (shuffle y, undef)"
2911///
2912/// into a single shuffle fed by a length-changing shuffle:
2913///
2914/// "shuffle x, (shuffle y, undef)"
2915///
2916/// Such chains arise e.g. from folding extract/insert sequences.
2917bool VectorCombine::foldShufflesOfLengthChangingShuffles(Instruction &I) {
2918 FixedVectorType *TrunkType = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
2919 if (!TrunkType)
2920 return false;
2921
2922 unsigned ChainLength = 0;
2923 SmallVector<int> Mask;
2924 SmallVector<int> YMask;
2925 InstructionCost OldCost = 0;
2926 InstructionCost NewCost = 0;
2927 Value *Trunk = &I;
2928 unsigned NumTrunkElts = TrunkType->getNumElements();
2929 Value *Y = nullptr;
2930
2931 for (;;) {
2932 // Match the current trunk against (commutations of) the pattern
2933 // "shuffle trunk', (shuffle y, undef)"
2934 ArrayRef<int> OuterMask;
2935 Value *OuterV0, *OuterV1;
2936 if (ChainLength != 0 && !Trunk->hasOneUse())
2937 break;
2938 if (!match(Trunk, m_Shuffle(m_Value(OuterV0), m_Value(OuterV1),
2939 m_Mask(OuterMask))))
2940 break;
2941 if (OuterV0->getType() != TrunkType) {
2942 // This shuffle is not length-preserving, so it cannot be part of the
2943 // chain.
2944 break;
2945 }
2946
2947 ArrayRef<int> InnerMask0, InnerMask1;
2948 Value *A0, *A1, *B0, *B1;
2949 bool Match0 =
2950 match(OuterV0, m_Shuffle(m_Value(A0), m_Value(B0), m_Mask(InnerMask0)));
2951 bool Match1 =
2952 match(OuterV1, m_Shuffle(m_Value(A1), m_Value(B1), m_Mask(InnerMask1)));
2953 bool Match0Leaf = Match0 && A0->getType() != I.getType();
2954 bool Match1Leaf = Match1 && A1->getType() != I.getType();
2955 if (Match0Leaf == Match1Leaf) {
2956 // Only handle the case of exactly one leaf in each step. The "two leaves"
2957 // case is handled by foldShuffleOfShuffles.
2958 break;
2959 }
2960
2961 SmallVector<int> CommutedOuterMask;
2962 if (Match0Leaf) {
2963 std::swap(OuterV0, OuterV1);
2964 std::swap(InnerMask0, InnerMask1);
2965 std::swap(A0, A1);
2966 std::swap(B0, B1);
2967 llvm::append_range(CommutedOuterMask, OuterMask);
2968 for (int &M : CommutedOuterMask) {
2969 if (M == PoisonMaskElem)
2970 continue;
2971 if (M < (int)NumTrunkElts)
2972 M += NumTrunkElts;
2973 else
2974 M -= NumTrunkElts;
2975 }
2976 OuterMask = CommutedOuterMask;
2977 }
2978 if (!OuterV1->hasOneUse())
2979 break;
2980
2981 if (!isa<UndefValue>(A1)) {
2982 if (!Y)
2983 Y = A1;
2984 else if (Y != A1)
2985 break;
2986 }
2987 if (!isa<UndefValue>(B1)) {
2988 if (!Y)
2989 Y = B1;
2990 else if (Y != B1)
2991 break;
2992 }
2993
2994 auto *YType = cast<FixedVectorType>(A1->getType());
2995 int NumLeafElts = YType->getNumElements();
2996 SmallVector<int> LocalYMask(InnerMask1);
2997 for (int &M : LocalYMask) {
2998 if (M >= NumLeafElts)
2999 M -= NumLeafElts;
3000 }
3001
3002 InstructionCost LocalOldCost =
3003 TTI.getInstructionCost(cast<User>(Trunk), CostKind) +
3004 TTI.getInstructionCost(cast<User>(OuterV1), CostKind);
3005
3006 // Handle the initial (start of chain) case.
3007 if (!ChainLength) {
3008 Mask.assign(OuterMask);
3009 YMask.assign(LocalYMask);
3010 OldCost = NewCost = LocalOldCost;
3011 Trunk = OuterV0;
3012 ChainLength++;
3013 continue;
3014 }
3015
3016 // For the non-root case, first attempt to combine masks.
3017 SmallVector<int> NewYMask(YMask);
3018 bool Valid = true;
3019 for (auto [CombinedM, LeafM] : llvm::zip(NewYMask, LocalYMask)) {
3020 if (LeafM == -1 || CombinedM == LeafM)
3021 continue;
3022 if (CombinedM == -1) {
3023 CombinedM = LeafM;
3024 } else {
3025 Valid = false;
3026 break;
3027 }
3028 }
3029 if (!Valid)
3030 break;
3031
3032 SmallVector<int> NewMask;
3033 NewMask.reserve(NumTrunkElts);
3034 for (int M : Mask) {
3035 if (M < 0 || M >= static_cast<int>(NumTrunkElts))
3036 NewMask.push_back(M);
3037 else
3038 NewMask.push_back(OuterMask[M]);
3039 }
3040
3041 // Break the chain if adding this new step complicates the shuffles such
3042 // that it would increase the new cost by more than the old cost of this
3043 // step.
3044 InstructionCost LocalNewCost =
3045 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc, TrunkType,
3046 YType, NewYMask, CostKind) +
3047 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, TrunkType,
3048 TrunkType, NewMask, CostKind);
3049
3050 if (LocalNewCost >= NewCost && LocalOldCost < LocalNewCost - NewCost)
3051 break;
3052
3053 LLVM_DEBUG({
3054 if (ChainLength == 1) {
3055 dbgs() << "Found chain of shuffles fed by length-changing shuffles: "
3056 << I << '\n';
3057 }
3058 dbgs() << " next chain link: " << *Trunk << '\n'
3059 << " old cost: " << (OldCost + LocalOldCost)
3060 << " new cost: " << LocalNewCost << '\n';
3061 });
3062
3063 Mask = NewMask;
3064 YMask = NewYMask;
3065 OldCost += LocalOldCost;
3066 NewCost = LocalNewCost;
3067 Trunk = OuterV0;
3068 ChainLength++;
3069 }
3070 if (ChainLength <= 1)
3071 return false;
3072
3073 if (llvm::all_of(Mask, [&](int M) {
3074 return M < 0 || M >= static_cast<int>(NumTrunkElts);
3075 })) {
3076 // Produce a canonical simplified form if all elements are sourced from Y.
3077 for (int &M : Mask) {
3078 if (M >= static_cast<int>(NumTrunkElts))
3079 M = YMask[M - NumTrunkElts];
3080 }
3081 Value *Root =
3082 Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Y, PoisonValue::get(Y->getType()), Mask);
3083 replaceValue(I, *Root);
3084 return true;
3085 }
3086
3087 Value *Leaf =
3088 Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Y, PoisonValue::get(Y->getType()), YMask);
3089 Value *Root = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Trunk, Leaf, Mask);
3090 replaceValue(I, *Root);
3091 return true;
3092}
3093
3094/// Try to convert
3095/// "shuffle (intrinsic), (intrinsic)" into "intrinsic (shuffle), (shuffle)".
3096bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleOfIntrinsics(Instruction &I) {
3097 Value *V0, *V1;
3098 ArrayRef<int> OldMask;
3099 if (!match(&I, m_Shuffle(m_Value(V0), m_Value(V1), m_Mask(OldMask))))
3100 return false;
3101
3102 auto *II0 = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(V0);
3103 auto *II1 = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(V1);
3104 if (!II0 || !II1)
3105 return false;
3106
3107 Intrinsic::ID IID = II0->getIntrinsicID();
3108 if (IID != II1->getIntrinsicID())
3109 return false;
3110 InstructionCost CostII0 =
3111 TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(IntrinsicCostAttributes(IID, *II0), CostKind);
3112 InstructionCost CostII1 =
3113 TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(IntrinsicCostAttributes(IID, *II1), CostKind);
3114
3115 auto *ShuffleDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
3116 auto *II0Ty = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(II0->getType());
3117 if (!ShuffleDstTy || !II0Ty)
3118 return false;
3119
3120 if (!isTriviallyVectorizable(IID))
3121 return false;
3122
3123 for (unsigned I = 0, E = II0->arg_size(); I != E; ++I)
3124 if (isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(IID, I, &TTI) &&
3125 II0->getArgOperand(I) != II1->getArgOperand(I))
3126 return false;
3127
3128 InstructionCost OldCost =
3129 CostII0 + CostII1 +
3130 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, ShuffleDstTy,
3131 II0Ty, OldMask, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {II0, II1}, &I);
3132
3133 SmallVector<Type *> NewArgsTy;
3134 InstructionCost NewCost = 0;
3135 SmallDenseSet<std::pair<Value *, Value *>> SeenOperandPairs;
3136 for (unsigned I = 0, E = II0->arg_size(); I != E; ++I) {
3137 if (isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(IID, I, &TTI)) {
3138 NewArgsTy.push_back(II0->getArgOperand(I)->getType());
3139 } else {
3140 auto *VecTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(II0->getArgOperand(I)->getType());
3141 auto *ArgTy = FixedVectorType::get(VecTy->getElementType(),
3142 ShuffleDstTy->getNumElements());
3143 NewArgsTy.push_back(ArgTy);
3144 std::pair<Value *, Value *> OperandPair =
3145 std::make_pair(II0->getArgOperand(I), II1->getArgOperand(I));
3146 if (!SeenOperandPairs.insert(OperandPair).second) {
3147 // We've already computed the cost for this operand pair.
3148 continue;
3149 }
3150 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(
3151 TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, ArgTy, VecTy, OldMask,
3152 CostKind, 0, nullptr, {II0->getArgOperand(I), II1->getArgOperand(I)});
3153 }
3154 }
3155 IntrinsicCostAttributes NewAttr(IID, ShuffleDstTy, NewArgsTy);
3156
3157 NewCost += TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(NewAttr, CostKind);
3158 if (!II0->hasOneUse())
3159 NewCost += CostII0;
3160 if (II1 != II0 && !II1->hasOneUse())
3161 NewCost += CostII1;
3162
3163 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a shuffle feeding two intrinsics: " << I
3164 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
3165 << "\n");
3166
3167 if (NewCost > OldCost)
3168 return false;
3169
3170 SmallVector<Value *> NewArgs;
3171 SmallDenseMap<std::pair<Value *, Value *>, Value *> ShuffleCache;
3172 for (unsigned I = 0, E = II0->arg_size(); I != E; ++I)
3173 if (isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(IID, I, &TTI)) {
3174 NewArgs.push_back(II0->getArgOperand(I));
3175 } else {
3176 std::pair<Value *, Value *> OperandPair =
3177 std::make_pair(II0->getArgOperand(I), II1->getArgOperand(I));
3178 auto It = ShuffleCache.find(OperandPair);
3179 if (It != ShuffleCache.end()) {
3180 // Reuse previously created shuffle for this operand pair.
3181 NewArgs.push_back(It->second);
3182 continue;
3183 }
3184 Value *Shuf = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(II0->getArgOperand(I),
3185 II1->getArgOperand(I), OldMask);
3186 ShuffleCache[OperandPair] = Shuf;
3187 NewArgs.push_back(Shuf);
3188 Worklist.pushValue(Shuf);
3189 }
3190 Value *NewIntrinsic = Builder.CreateIntrinsic(ShuffleDstTy, IID, NewArgs);
3191
3192 // Intersect flags from the old intrinsics.
3193 if (auto *NewInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(NewIntrinsic)) {
3194 NewInst->copyIRFlags(II0);
3195 NewInst->andIRFlags(II1);
3196 }
3197
3198 replaceValue(I, *NewIntrinsic);
3199 return true;
3200}
3201
3202/// Try to convert
3203/// "shuffle (intrinsic), (poison/undef)" into "intrinsic (shuffle)".
3204bool VectorCombine::foldPermuteOfIntrinsic(Instruction &I) {
3205 Value *V0;
3206 ArrayRef<int> Mask;
3207 if (!match(&I, m_Shuffle(m_Value(V0), m_Undef(), m_Mask(Mask))))
3208 return false;
3209
3210 auto *II0 = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(V0);
3211 if (!II0)
3212 return false;
3213
3214 auto *ShuffleDstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
3215 auto *IntrinsicSrcTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(II0->getType());
3216 if (!ShuffleDstTy || !IntrinsicSrcTy)
3217 return false;
3218
3219 // Validate it's a pure permute, mask should only reference the first vector
3220 unsigned NumSrcElts = IntrinsicSrcTy->getNumElements();
3221 if (any_of(Mask, [NumSrcElts](int M) { return M >= (int)NumSrcElts; }))
3222 return false;
3223
3224 Intrinsic::ID IID = II0->getIntrinsicID();
3225 if (!isTriviallyVectorizable(IID))
3226 return false;
3227
3228 // Cost analysis
3229 InstructionCost IntrinsicCost =
3230 TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(IntrinsicCostAttributes(IID, *II0), CostKind);
3231 InstructionCost OldCost =
3232 IntrinsicCost +
3233 TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc, ShuffleDstTy,
3234 IntrinsicSrcTy, Mask, CostKind, 0, nullptr, {V0}, &I);
3235
3236 SmallVector<Type *> NewArgsTy;
3237 InstructionCost NewCost = 0;
3238 for (unsigned I = 0, E = II0->arg_size(); I != E; ++I) {
3239 if (isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(IID, I, &TTI)) {
3240 NewArgsTy.push_back(II0->getArgOperand(I)->getType());
3241 } else {
3242 auto *VecTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(II0->getArgOperand(I)->getType());
3243 auto *ArgTy = FixedVectorType::get(VecTy->getElementType(),
3244 ShuffleDstTy->getNumElements());
3245 NewArgsTy.push_back(ArgTy);
3246 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc,
3247 ArgTy, VecTy, Mask, CostKind, 0, nullptr,
3248 {II0->getArgOperand(I)});
3249 }
3250 }
3251 IntrinsicCostAttributes NewAttr(IID, ShuffleDstTy, NewArgsTy);
3252 NewCost += TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(NewAttr, CostKind);
3253
3254 // If the intrinsic has multiple uses, we need to account for the cost of
3255 // keeping the original intrinsic around.
3256 if (!II0->hasOneUse())
3257 NewCost += IntrinsicCost;
3258
3259 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a permute of intrinsic: " << I << "\n OldCost: "
3260 << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost << "\n");
3261
3262 if (NewCost > OldCost)
3263 return false;
3264
3265 // Transform
3266 SmallVector<Value *> NewArgs;
3267 for (unsigned I = 0, E = II0->arg_size(); I != E; ++I) {
3268 if (isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(IID, I, &TTI)) {
3269 NewArgs.push_back(II0->getArgOperand(I));
3270 } else {
3271 Value *Shuf = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(II0->getArgOperand(I), Mask);
3272 NewArgs.push_back(Shuf);
3273 Worklist.pushValue(Shuf);
3274 }
3275 }
3276
3277 Value *NewIntrinsic = Builder.CreateIntrinsic(ShuffleDstTy, IID, NewArgs);
3278
3279 if (auto *NewInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(NewIntrinsic))
3280 NewInst->copyIRFlags(II0);
3281
3282 replaceValue(I, *NewIntrinsic);
3283 return true;
3284}
3285
3286using InstLane = std::pair<Use *, int>;
3287
3288static InstLane lookThroughShuffles(Use *U, int Lane) {
3289 while (auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(U->get())) {
3290 unsigned NumElts =
3291 cast<FixedVectorType>(SV->getOperand(0)->getType())->getNumElements();
3292 int M = SV->getMaskValue(Lane);
3293 if (M < 0)
3294 return {nullptr, PoisonMaskElem};
3295 if (static_cast<unsigned>(M) < NumElts) {
3296 U = &SV->getOperandUse(0);
3297 Lane = M;
3298 } else {
3299 U = &SV->getOperandUse(1);
3300 Lane = M - NumElts;
3301 }
3302 }
3303 return InstLane{U, Lane};
3304}
3305
3306static SmallVector<InstLane>
3307generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(ArrayRef<InstLane> Item, int Op) {
3308 SmallVector<InstLane> NItem;
3309 for (InstLane IL : Item) {
3310 auto [U, Lane] = IL;
3311 InstLane OpLane =
3312 U ? lookThroughShuffles(&cast<Instruction>(U->get())->getOperandUse(Op),
3313 Lane)
3314 : InstLane{nullptr, PoisonMaskElem};
3315 NItem.emplace_back(OpLane);
3316 }
3317 return NItem;
3318}
3319
3320/// Detect concat of multiple values into a vector
3321static bool isFreeConcat(ArrayRef<InstLane> Item, TTI::TargetCostKind CostKind,
3322 const TargetTransformInfo &TTI) {
3323 auto *Ty = cast<FixedVectorType>(Item.front().first->get()->getType());
3324 unsigned NumElts = Ty->getNumElements();
3325 if (Item.size() == NumElts || NumElts == 1 || Item.size() % NumElts != 0)
3326 return false;
3327
3328 // Check that the concat is free, usually meaning that the type will be split
3329 // during legalization.
3330 SmallVector<int, 16> ConcatMask(NumElts * 2);
3331 std::iota(ConcatMask.begin(), ConcatMask.end(), 0);
3332 if (TTI.getShuffleCost(TTI::SK_PermuteTwoSrc,
3333 FixedVectorType::get(Ty->getScalarType(), NumElts * 2),
3334 Ty, ConcatMask, CostKind) != 0)
3335 return false;
3336
3337 unsigned NumSlices = Item.size() / NumElts;
3338 // Currently we generate a tree of shuffles for the concats, which limits us
3339 // to a power2.
3340 if (!isPowerOf2_32(NumSlices))
3341 return false;
3342 for (unsigned Slice = 0; Slice < NumSlices; ++Slice) {
3343 Use *SliceV = Item[Slice * NumElts].first;
3344 if (!SliceV || SliceV->get()->getType() != Ty)
3345 return false;
3346 for (unsigned Elt = 0; Elt < NumElts; ++Elt) {
3347 auto [V, Lane] = Item[Slice * NumElts + Elt];
3348 if (Lane != static_cast<int>(Elt) || SliceV->get() != V->get())
3349 return false;
3350 }
3351 }
3352 return true;
3353}
3354
3355static Value *generateNewInstTree(ArrayRef<InstLane> Item, FixedVectorType *Ty,
3356 const SmallPtrSet<Use *, 4> &IdentityLeafs,
3357 const SmallPtrSet<Use *, 4> &SplatLeafs,
3358 const SmallPtrSet<Use *, 4> &ConcatLeafs,
3359 IRBuilderBase &Builder,
3360 const TargetTransformInfo *TTI) {
3361 auto [FrontU, FrontLane] = Item.front();
3362
3363 if (IdentityLeafs.contains(FrontU)) {
3364 return FrontU->get();
3365 }
3366 if (SplatLeafs.contains(FrontU)) {
3367 SmallVector<int, 16> Mask(Ty->getNumElements(), FrontLane);
3368 return Builder.CreateShuffleVector(FrontU->get(), Mask);
3369 }
3370 if (ConcatLeafs.contains(FrontU)) {
3371 unsigned NumElts =
3372 cast<FixedVectorType>(FrontU->get()->getType())->getNumElements();
3373 SmallVector<Value *> Values(Item.size() / NumElts, nullptr);
3374 for (unsigned S = 0; S < Values.size(); ++S)
3375 Values[S] = Item[S * NumElts].first->get();
3376
3377 while (Values.size() > 1) {
3378 NumElts *= 2;
3379 SmallVector<int, 16> Mask(NumElts, 0);
3380 std::iota(Mask.begin(), Mask.end(), 0);
3381 SmallVector<Value *> NewValues(Values.size() / 2, nullptr);
3382 for (unsigned S = 0; S < NewValues.size(); ++S)
3383 NewValues[S] =
3384 Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Values[S * 2], Values[S * 2 + 1], Mask);
3385 Values = NewValues;
3386 }
3387 return Values[0];
3388 }
3389
3390 auto *I = cast<Instruction>(FrontU->get());
3391 auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(I);
3392 unsigned NumOps = I->getNumOperands() - (II ? 1 : 0);
3393 SmallVector<Value *> Ops(NumOps);
3394 for (unsigned Idx = 0; Idx < NumOps; Idx++) {
3395 if (II &&
3396 isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(II->getIntrinsicID(), Idx, TTI)) {
3397 Ops[Idx] = II->getOperand(Idx);
3398 continue;
3399 }
3400 Ops[Idx] = generateNewInstTree(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, Idx),
3401 Ty, IdentityLeafs, SplatLeafs, ConcatLeafs,
3402 Builder, TTI);
3403 }
3404
3405 SmallVector<Value *, 8> ValueList;
3406 for (const auto &Lane : Item)
3407 if (Lane.first)
3408 ValueList.push_back(Lane.first->get());
3409
3410 Type *DstTy =
3411 FixedVectorType::get(I->getType()->getScalarType(), Ty->getNumElements());
3412 if (auto *BI = dyn_cast<BinaryOperator>(I)) {
3413 auto *Value = Builder.CreateBinOp((Instruction::BinaryOps)BI->getOpcode(),
3414 Ops[0], Ops[1]);
3415 propagateIRFlags(Value, ValueList);
3416 return Value;
3417 }
3418 if (auto *CI = dyn_cast<CmpInst>(I)) {
3419 auto *Value = Builder.CreateCmp(CI->getPredicate(), Ops[0], Ops[1]);
3420 propagateIRFlags(Value, ValueList);
3421 return Value;
3422 }
3423 if (auto *SI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(I)) {
3424 auto *Value = Builder.CreateSelect(Ops[0], Ops[1], Ops[2], "", SI);
3425 propagateIRFlags(Value, ValueList);
3426 return Value;
3427 }
3428 if (auto *CI = dyn_cast<CastInst>(I)) {
3429 auto *Value = Builder.CreateCast(CI->getOpcode(), Ops[0], DstTy);
3430 propagateIRFlags(Value, ValueList);
3431 return Value;
3432 }
3433 if (II) {
3434 auto *Value = Builder.CreateIntrinsic(DstTy, II->getIntrinsicID(), Ops);
3435 propagateIRFlags(Value, ValueList);
3436 return Value;
3437 }
3438 assert(isa<UnaryInstruction>(I) && "Unexpected instruction type in Generate");
3439 auto *Value =
3440 Builder.CreateUnOp((Instruction::UnaryOps)I->getOpcode(), Ops[0]);
3441 propagateIRFlags(Value, ValueList);
3442 return Value;
3443}
3444
3445// Starting from a shuffle, look up through operands tracking the shuffled index
3446// of each lane. If we can simplify away the shuffles to identities then
3447// do so.
3448bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleToIdentity(Instruction &I) {
3449 auto *Ty = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
3450 if (!Ty || I.use_empty())
3451 return false;
3452
3453 SmallVector<InstLane> Start(Ty->getNumElements());
3454 for (unsigned M = 0, E = Ty->getNumElements(); M < E; ++M)
3455 Start[M] = lookThroughShuffles(&*I.use_begin(), M);
3456
3457 SmallVector<SmallVector<InstLane>> Worklist;
3458 Worklist.push_back(Start);
3459 SmallPtrSet<Use *, 4> IdentityLeafs, SplatLeafs, ConcatLeafs;
3460 unsigned NumVisited = 0;
3461
3462 while (!Worklist.empty()) {
3463 if (++NumVisited > MaxInstrsToScan)
3464 return false;
3465
3466 SmallVector<InstLane> Item = Worklist.pop_back_val();
3467 auto [FrontU, FrontLane] = Item.front();
3468
3469 // If we found an undef first lane then bail out to keep things simple.
3470 if (!FrontU)
3471 return false;
3472
3473 // Helper to peek through bitcasts to the same value.
3474 auto IsEquiv = [&](Value *X, Value *Y) {
3475 return X->getType() == Y->getType() &&
3476 peekThroughBitcasts(X) == peekThroughBitcasts(Y);
3477 };
3478
3479 // Look for an identity value.
3480 if (FrontLane == 0 &&
3481 cast<FixedVectorType>(FrontU->get()->getType())->getNumElements() ==
3482 Ty->getNumElements() &&
3483 all_of(drop_begin(enumerate(Item)), [IsEquiv, Item](const auto &E) {
3484 Value *FrontV = Item.front().first->get();
3485 return !E.value().first || (IsEquiv(E.value().first->get(), FrontV) &&
3486 E.value().second == (int)E.index());
3487 })) {
3488 IdentityLeafs.insert(FrontU);
3489 continue;
3490 }
3491 // Look for constants, for the moment only supporting constant splats.
3492 if (auto *C = dyn_cast<Constant>(FrontU);
3493 C && C->getSplatValue() &&
3494 all_of(drop_begin(Item), [Item](InstLane &IL) {
3495 Value *FrontV = Item.front().first->get();
3496 Use *U = IL.first;
3497 return !U || (isa<Constant>(U->get()) &&
3498 cast<Constant>(U->get())->getSplatValue() ==
3499 cast<Constant>(FrontV)->getSplatValue());
3500 })) {
3501 SplatLeafs.insert(FrontU);
3502 continue;
3503 }
3504 // Look for a splat value.
3505 if (all_of(drop_begin(Item), [Item](InstLane &IL) {
3506 auto [FrontU, FrontLane] = Item.front();
3507 auto [U, Lane] = IL;
3508 return !U || (U->get() == FrontU->get() && Lane == FrontLane);
3509 })) {
3510 SplatLeafs.insert(FrontU);
3511 continue;
3512 }
3513
3514 // We need each element to be the same type of value, and check that each
3515 // element has a single use.
3516 auto CheckLaneIsEquivalentToFirst = [Item](InstLane IL) {
3517 Value *FrontV = Item.front().first->get();
3518 if (!IL.first)
3519 return true;
3520 Value *V = IL.first->get();
3521 if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V); I && !I->hasOneUser())
3522 return false;
3523 if (V->getValueID() != FrontV->getValueID())
3524 return false;
3525 if (auto *CI = dyn_cast<CmpInst>(V))
3526 if (CI->getPredicate() != cast<CmpInst>(FrontV)->getPredicate())
3527 return false;
3528 if (auto *CI = dyn_cast<CastInst>(V))
3529 if (CI->getSrcTy()->getScalarType() !=
3530 cast<CastInst>(FrontV)->getSrcTy()->getScalarType())
3531 return false;
3532 if (auto *SI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(V))
3533 if (!isa<VectorType>(SI->getOperand(0)->getType()) ||
3534 SI->getOperand(0)->getType() !=
3535 cast<SelectInst>(FrontV)->getOperand(0)->getType())
3536 return false;
3537 if (isa<CallInst>(V) && !isa<IntrinsicInst>(V))
3538 return false;
3539 auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(V);
3540 return !II || (isa<IntrinsicInst>(FrontV) &&
3541 II->getIntrinsicID() ==
3542 cast<IntrinsicInst>(FrontV)->getIntrinsicID() &&
3543 !II->hasOperandBundles());
3544 };
3545 if (all_of(drop_begin(Item), CheckLaneIsEquivalentToFirst)) {
3546 // Check the operator is one that we support.
3547 if (isa<BinaryOperator, CmpInst>(FrontU)) {
3548 // We exclude div/rem in case they hit UB from poison lanes.
3549 if (auto *BO = dyn_cast<BinaryOperator>(FrontU);
3550 BO && BO->isIntDivRem())
3551 return false;
3552 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 0));
3553 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 1));
3554 continue;
3555 } else if (isa<UnaryOperator, TruncInst, ZExtInst, SExtInst, FPToSIInst,
3556 FPToUIInst, SIToFPInst, UIToFPInst>(FrontU)) {
3557 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 0));
3558 continue;
3559 } else if (auto *BitCast = dyn_cast<BitCastInst>(FrontU)) {
3560 // TODO: Handle vector widening/narrowing bitcasts.
3561 auto *DstTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(BitCast->getDestTy());
3562 auto *SrcTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(BitCast->getSrcTy());
3563 if (DstTy && SrcTy &&
3564 SrcTy->getNumElements() == DstTy->getNumElements()) {
3565 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 0));
3566 continue;
3567 }
3568 } else if (isa<SelectInst>(FrontU)) {
3569 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 0));
3570 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 1));
3571 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, 2));
3572 continue;
3573 } else if (auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(FrontU);
3574 II && isTriviallyVectorizable(II->getIntrinsicID()) &&
3575 !II->hasOperandBundles()) {
3576 for (unsigned Op = 0, E = II->getNumOperands() - 1; Op < E; Op++) {
3577 if (isVectorIntrinsicWithScalarOpAtArg(II->getIntrinsicID(), Op,
3578 &TTI)) {
3579 if (!all_of(drop_begin(Item), [Item, Op](InstLane &IL) {
3580 Value *FrontV = Item.front().first->get();
3581 Use *U = IL.first;
3582 return !U || (cast<Instruction>(U->get())->getOperand(Op) ==
3583 cast<Instruction>(FrontV)->getOperand(Op));
3584 }))
3585 return false;
3586 continue;
3587 }
3588 Worklist.push_back(generateInstLaneVectorFromOperand(Item, Op));
3589 }
3590 continue;
3591 }
3592 }
3593
3594 if (isFreeConcat(Item, CostKind, TTI)) {
3595 ConcatLeafs.insert(FrontU);
3596 continue;
3597 }
3598
3599 return false;
3600 }
3601
3602 if (NumVisited <= 1)
3603 return false;
3604
3605 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a superfluous identity shuffle: " << I << "\n");
3606
3607 // If we got this far, we know the shuffles are superfluous and can be
3608 // removed. Scan through again and generate the new tree of instructions.
3609 Builder.SetInsertPoint(&I);
3610 Value *V = generateNewInstTree(Start, Ty, IdentityLeafs, SplatLeafs,
3611 ConcatLeafs, Builder, &TTI);
3612 replaceValue(I, *V);
3613 return true;
3614}
3615
3616/// Given a commutative reduction, the order of the input lanes does not alter
3617/// the results. We can use this to remove certain shuffles feeding the
3618/// reduction, removing the need to shuffle at all.
3619bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleFromReductions(Instruction &I) {
3620 auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(&I);
3621 if (!II)
3622 return false;
3623 switch (II->getIntrinsicID()) {
3624 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_add:
3625 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_mul:
3626 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_and:
3627 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_or:
3628 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_xor:
3629 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_smin:
3630 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_smax:
3631 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_umin:
3632 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_umax:
3633 break;
3634 default:
3635 return false;
3636 }
3637
3638 // Find all the inputs when looking through operations that do not alter the
3639 // lane order (binops, for example). Currently we look for a single shuffle,
3640 // and can ignore splat values.
3641 std::queue<Value *> Worklist;
3642 SmallPtrSet<Value *, 4> Visited;
3643 ShuffleVectorInst *Shuffle = nullptr;
3644 if (auto *Op = dyn_cast<Instruction>(I.getOperand(0)))
3645 Worklist.push(Op);
3646
3647 while (!Worklist.empty()) {
3648 Value *CV = Worklist.front();
3649 Worklist.pop();
3650 if (Visited.contains(CV))
3651 continue;
3652
3653 // Splats don't change the order, so can be safely ignored.
3654 if (isSplatValue(CV))
3655 continue;
3656
3657 Visited.insert(CV);
3658
3659 if (auto *CI = dyn_cast<Instruction>(CV)) {
3660 if (CI->isBinaryOp()) {
3661 for (auto *Op : CI->operand_values())
3662 Worklist.push(Op);
3663 continue;
3664 } else if (auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(CI)) {
3665 if (Shuffle && Shuffle != SV)
3666 return false;
3667 Shuffle = SV;
3668 continue;
3669 }
3670 }
3671
3672 // Anything else is currently an unknown node.
3673 return false;
3674 }
3675
3676 if (!Shuffle)
3677 return false;
3678
3679 // Check all uses of the binary ops and shuffles are also included in the
3680 // lane-invariant operations (Visited should be the list of lanewise
3681 // instructions, including the shuffle that we found).
3682 for (auto *V : Visited)
3683 for (auto *U : V->users())
3684 if (!Visited.contains(U) && U != &I)
3685 return false;
3686
3687 FixedVectorType *VecType =
3688 dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(II->getOperand(0)->getType());
3689 if (!VecType)
3690 return false;
3691 FixedVectorType *ShuffleInputType =
3692 dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(Shuffle->getOperand(0)->getType());
3693 if (!ShuffleInputType)
3694 return false;
3695 unsigned NumInputElts = ShuffleInputType->getNumElements();
3696
3697 // Find the mask from sorting the lanes into order. This is most likely to
3698 // become a identity or concat mask. Undef elements are pushed to the end.
3699 SmallVector<int> ConcatMask;
3700 Shuffle->getShuffleMask(ConcatMask);
3701 sort(ConcatMask, [](int X, int Y) { return (unsigned)X < (unsigned)Y; });
3702 bool UsesSecondVec =
3703 any_of(ConcatMask, [&](int M) { return M >= (int)NumInputElts; });
3704
3705 InstructionCost OldCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(
3706 UsesSecondVec ? TTI::SK_PermuteTwoSrc : TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc, VecType,
3707 ShuffleInputType, Shuffle->getShuffleMask(), CostKind);
3708 InstructionCost NewCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(
3709 UsesSecondVec ? TTI::SK_PermuteTwoSrc : TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc, VecType,
3710 ShuffleInputType, ConcatMask, CostKind);
3711
3712 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a reduction feeding from a shuffle: " << *Shuffle
3713 << "\n");
3714 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << " OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
3715 << "\n");
3716 bool MadeChanges = false;
3717 if (NewCost < OldCost) {
3718 Builder.SetInsertPoint(Shuffle);
3719 Value *NewShuffle = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(
3720 Shuffle->getOperand(0), Shuffle->getOperand(1), ConcatMask);
3721 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Created new shuffle: " << *NewShuffle << "\n");
3722 replaceValue(*Shuffle, *NewShuffle);
3723 return true;
3724 }
3725
3726 // See if we can re-use foldSelectShuffle, getting it to reduce the size of
3727 // the shuffle into a nicer order, as it can ignore the order of the shuffles.
3728 MadeChanges |= foldSelectShuffle(*Shuffle, true);
3729 return MadeChanges;
3730}
3731
3732/// For a given chain of patterns of the following form:
3733///
3734/// ```
3735/// %1 = shufflevector <n x ty1> %0, <n x ty1> poison <n x ty2> mask
3736///
3737/// %2 = tail call <n x ty1> llvm.<umin/umax/smin/smax>(<n x ty1> %0, <n x
3738/// ty1> %1)
3739/// OR
3740/// %2 = add/mul/or/and/xor <n x ty1> %0, %1
3741///
3742/// %3 = shufflevector <n x ty1> %2, <n x ty1> poison <n x ty2> mask
3743/// ...
3744/// ...
3745/// %(i - 1) = tail call <n x ty1> llvm.<umin/umax/smin/smax>(<n x ty1> %(i -
3746/// 3), <n x ty1> %(i - 2)
3747/// OR
3748/// %(i - 1) = add/mul/or/and/xor <n x ty1> %(i - 3), %(i - 2)
3749///
3750/// %(i) = extractelement <n x ty1> %(i - 1), 0
3751/// ```
3752///
3753/// Where:
3754/// `mask` follows a partition pattern:
3755///
3756/// Ex:
3757/// [n = 8, p = poison]
3758///
3759/// 4 5 6 7 | p p p p
3760/// 2 3 | p p p p p p
3761/// 1 | p p p p p p p
3762///
3763/// For powers of 2, there's a consistent pattern, but for other cases
3764/// the parity of the current half value at each step decides the
3765/// next partition half (see `ExpectedParityMask` for more logical details
3766/// in generalising this).
3767///
3768/// Ex:
3769/// [n = 6]
3770///
3771/// 3 4 5 | p p p
3772/// 1 2 | p p p p
3773/// 1 | p p p p p
3774bool VectorCombine::foldShuffleChainsToReduce(Instruction &I) {
3775 // Going bottom-up for the pattern.
3776 std::queue<Value *> InstWorklist;
3777 InstructionCost OrigCost = 0;
3778
3779 // Common instruction operation after each shuffle op.
3780 std::optional<unsigned int> CommonCallOp = std::nullopt;
3781 std::optional<Instruction::BinaryOps> CommonBinOp = std::nullopt;
3782
3783 bool IsFirstCallOrBinInst = true;
3784 bool ShouldBeCallOrBinInst = true;
3785
3786 // This stores the last used instructions for shuffle/common op.
3787 //
3788 // PrevVecV[0] / PrevVecV[1] store the last two simultaneous
3789 // instructions from either shuffle/common op.
3790 SmallVector<Value *, 2> PrevVecV(2, nullptr);
3791
3792 Value *VecOpEE;
3793 if (!match(&I, m_ExtractElt(m_Value(VecOpEE), m_Zero())))
3794 return false;
3795
3796 auto *FVT = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(VecOpEE->getType());
3797 if (!FVT)
3798 return false;
3799
3800 int64_t VecSize = FVT->getNumElements();
3801 if (VecSize < 2)
3802 return false;
3803
3804 // Number of levels would be ~log2(n), considering we always partition
3805 // by half for this fold pattern.
3806 unsigned int NumLevels = Log2_64_Ceil(VecSize), VisitedCnt = 0;
3807 int64_t ShuffleMaskHalf = 1, ExpectedParityMask = 0;
3808
3809 // This is how we generalise for all element sizes.
3810 // At each step, if vector size is odd, we need non-poison
3811 // values to cover the dominant half so we don't miss out on any element.
3812 //
3813 // This mask will help us retrieve this as we go from bottom to top:
3814 //
3815 // Mask Set -> N = N * 2 - 1
3816 // Mask Unset -> N = N * 2
3817 for (int Cur = VecSize, Mask = NumLevels - 1; Cur > 1;
3818 Cur = (Cur + 1) / 2, --Mask) {
3819 if (Cur & 1)
3820 ExpectedParityMask |= (1ll << Mask);
3821 }
3822
3823 InstWorklist.push(VecOpEE);
3824
3825 while (!InstWorklist.empty()) {
3826 Value *CI = InstWorklist.front();
3827 InstWorklist.pop();
3828
3829 if (auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(CI)) {
3830 if (!ShouldBeCallOrBinInst)
3831 return false;
3832
3833 if (!IsFirstCallOrBinInst &&
3834 any_of(PrevVecV, [](Value *VecV) { return VecV == nullptr; }))
3835 return false;
3836
3837 // For the first found call/bin op, the vector has to come from the
3838 // extract element op.
3839 if (II != (IsFirstCallOrBinInst ? VecOpEE : PrevVecV[0]))
3840 return false;
3841 IsFirstCallOrBinInst = false;
3842
3843 if (!CommonCallOp)
3844 CommonCallOp = II->getIntrinsicID();
3845 if (II->getIntrinsicID() != *CommonCallOp)
3846 return false;
3847
3848 switch (II->getIntrinsicID()) {
3849 case Intrinsic::umin:
3850 case Intrinsic::umax:
3851 case Intrinsic::smin:
3852 case Intrinsic::smax: {
3853 auto *Op0 = II->getOperand(0);
3854 auto *Op1 = II->getOperand(1);
3855 PrevVecV[0] = Op0;
3856 PrevVecV[1] = Op1;
3857 break;
3858 }
3859 default:
3860 return false;
3861 }
3862 ShouldBeCallOrBinInst ^= 1;
3863
3864 IntrinsicCostAttributes ICA(
3865 *CommonCallOp, II->getType(),
3866 {PrevVecV[0]->getType(), PrevVecV[1]->getType()});
3867 OrigCost += TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(ICA, CostKind);
3868
3869 // We may need a swap here since it can be (a, b) or (b, a)
3870 // and accordingly change as we go up.
3871 if (!isa<ShuffleVectorInst>(PrevVecV[1]))
3872 std::swap(PrevVecV[0], PrevVecV[1]);
3873 InstWorklist.push(PrevVecV[1]);
3874 InstWorklist.push(PrevVecV[0]);
3875 } else if (auto *BinOp = dyn_cast<BinaryOperator>(CI)) {
3876 // Similar logic for bin ops.
3877
3878 if (!ShouldBeCallOrBinInst)
3879 return false;
3880
3881 if (!IsFirstCallOrBinInst &&
3882 any_of(PrevVecV, [](Value *VecV) { return VecV == nullptr; }))
3883 return false;
3884
3885 if (BinOp != (IsFirstCallOrBinInst ? VecOpEE : PrevVecV[0]))
3886 return false;
3887 IsFirstCallOrBinInst = false;
3888
3889 if (!CommonBinOp)
3890 CommonBinOp = BinOp->getOpcode();
3891
3892 if (BinOp->getOpcode() != *CommonBinOp)
3893 return false;
3894
3895 switch (*CommonBinOp) {
3896 case BinaryOperator::Add:
3897 case BinaryOperator::Mul:
3898 case BinaryOperator::Or:
3899 case BinaryOperator::And:
3900 case BinaryOperator::Xor: {
3901 auto *Op0 = BinOp->getOperand(0);
3902 auto *Op1 = BinOp->getOperand(1);
3903 PrevVecV[0] = Op0;
3904 PrevVecV[1] = Op1;
3905 break;
3906 }
3907 default:
3908 return false;
3909 }
3910 ShouldBeCallOrBinInst ^= 1;
3911
3912 OrigCost +=
3913 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(*CommonBinOp, BinOp->getType(), CostKind);
3914
3915 if (!isa<ShuffleVectorInst>(PrevVecV[1]))
3916 std::swap(PrevVecV[0], PrevVecV[1]);
3917 InstWorklist.push(PrevVecV[1]);
3918 InstWorklist.push(PrevVecV[0]);
3919 } else if (auto *SVInst = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(CI)) {
3920 // We shouldn't have any null values in the previous vectors,
3921 // is so, there was a mismatch in pattern.
3922 if (ShouldBeCallOrBinInst ||
3923 any_of(PrevVecV, [](Value *VecV) { return VecV == nullptr; }))
3924 return false;
3925
3926 if (SVInst != PrevVecV[1])
3927 return false;
3928
3929 ArrayRef<int> CurMask;
3930 if (!match(SVInst, m_Shuffle(m_Specific(PrevVecV[0]), m_Poison(),
3931 m_Mask(CurMask))))
3932 return false;
3933
3934 // Subtract the parity mask when checking the condition.
3935 for (int Mask = 0, MaskSize = CurMask.size(); Mask != MaskSize; ++Mask) {
3936 if (Mask < ShuffleMaskHalf &&
3937 CurMask[Mask] != ShuffleMaskHalf + Mask - (ExpectedParityMask & 1))
3938 return false;
3939 if (Mask >= ShuffleMaskHalf && CurMask[Mask] != -1)
3940 return false;
3941 }
3942
3943 // Update mask values.
3944 ShuffleMaskHalf *= 2;
3945 ShuffleMaskHalf -= (ExpectedParityMask & 1);
3946 ExpectedParityMask >>= 1;
3947
3948 OrigCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc,
3949 SVInst->getType(), SVInst->getType(),
3950 CurMask, CostKind);
3951
3952 VisitedCnt += 1;
3953 if (!ExpectedParityMask && VisitedCnt == NumLevels)
3954 break;
3955
3956 ShouldBeCallOrBinInst ^= 1;
3957 } else {
3958 return false;
3959 }
3960 }
3961
3962 // Pattern should end with a shuffle op.
3963 if (ShouldBeCallOrBinInst)
3964 return false;
3965
3966 assert(VecSize != -1 && "Expected Match for Vector Size");
3967
3968 Value *FinalVecV = PrevVecV[0];
3969 if (!FinalVecV)
3970 return false;
3971
3972 auto *FinalVecVTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(FinalVecV->getType());
3973
3974 Intrinsic::ID ReducedOp =
3975 (CommonCallOp ? getMinMaxReductionIntrinsicID(*CommonCallOp)
3976 : getReductionForBinop(*CommonBinOp));
3977 if (!ReducedOp)
3978 return false;
3979
3980 IntrinsicCostAttributes ICA(ReducedOp, FinalVecVTy, {FinalVecV});
3981 InstructionCost NewCost = TTI.getIntrinsicInstrCost(ICA, CostKind);
3982
3983 if (NewCost >= OrigCost)
3984 return false;
3985
3986 auto *ReducedResult =
3987 Builder.CreateIntrinsic(ReducedOp, {FinalVecV->getType()}, {FinalVecV});
3988 replaceValue(I, *ReducedResult);
3989
3990 return true;
3991}
3992
3993/// Determine if its more efficient to fold:
3994/// reduce(trunc(x)) -> trunc(reduce(x)).
3995/// reduce(sext(x)) -> sext(reduce(x)).
3996/// reduce(zext(x)) -> zext(reduce(x)).
3997bool VectorCombine::foldCastFromReductions(Instruction &I) {
3998 auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(&I);
3999 if (!II)
4000 return false;
4001
4002 bool TruncOnly = false;
4003 Intrinsic::ID IID = II->getIntrinsicID();
4004 switch (IID) {
4005 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_add:
4006 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_mul:
4007 TruncOnly = true;
4008 break;
4009 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_and:
4010 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_or:
4011 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_xor:
4012 break;
4013 default:
4014 return false;
4015 }
4016
4017 unsigned ReductionOpc = getArithmeticReductionInstruction(IID);
4018 Value *ReductionSrc = I.getOperand(0);
4019
4020 Value *Src;
4021 if (!match(ReductionSrc, m_OneUse(m_Trunc(m_Value(Src)))) &&
4022 (TruncOnly || !match(ReductionSrc, m_OneUse(m_ZExtOrSExt(m_Value(Src))))))
4023 return false;
4024
4025 auto CastOpc =
4026 (Instruction::CastOps)cast<Instruction>(ReductionSrc)->getOpcode();
4027
4028 auto *SrcTy = cast<VectorType>(Src->getType());
4029 auto *ReductionSrcTy = cast<VectorType>(ReductionSrc->getType());
4030 Type *ResultTy = I.getType();
4031
4032 InstructionCost OldCost = TTI.getArithmeticReductionCost(
4033 ReductionOpc, ReductionSrcTy, std::nullopt, CostKind);
4034 OldCost += TTI.getCastInstrCost(CastOpc, ReductionSrcTy, SrcTy,
4035 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind,
4036 cast<CastInst>(ReductionSrc));
4037 InstructionCost NewCost =
4038 TTI.getArithmeticReductionCost(ReductionOpc, SrcTy, std::nullopt,
4039 CostKind) +
4040 TTI.getCastInstrCost(CastOpc, ResultTy, ReductionSrcTy->getScalarType(),
4041 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
4042
4043 if (OldCost <= NewCost || !NewCost.isValid())
4044 return false;
4045
4046 Value *NewReduction = Builder.CreateIntrinsic(SrcTy->getScalarType(),
4047 II->getIntrinsicID(), {Src});
4048 Value *NewCast = Builder.CreateCast(CastOpc, NewReduction, ResultTy);
4049 replaceValue(I, *NewCast);
4050 return true;
4051}
4052
4053/// Returns true if this ShuffleVectorInst eventually feeds into a
4054/// vector reduction intrinsic (e.g., vector_reduce_add) by only following
4055/// chains of shuffles and binary operators (in any combination/order).
4056/// The search does not go deeper than the given Depth.
4057static bool feedsIntoVectorReduction(ShuffleVectorInst *SVI) {
4058 constexpr unsigned MaxVisited = 32;
4059 SmallPtrSet<Instruction *, 8> Visited;
4060 SmallVector<Instruction *, 4> WorkList;
4061 bool FoundReduction = false;
4062
4063 WorkList.push_back(SVI);
4064 while (!WorkList.empty()) {
4065 Instruction *I = WorkList.pop_back_val();
4066 for (User *U : I->users()) {
4067 auto *UI = cast<Instruction>(U);
4068 if (!UI || !Visited.insert(UI).second)
4069 continue;
4070 if (Visited.size() > MaxVisited)
4071 return false;
4072 if (auto *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(UI)) {
4073 // More than one reduction reached
4074 if (FoundReduction)
4075 return false;
4076 switch (II->getIntrinsicID()) {
4077 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_add:
4078 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_mul:
4079 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_and:
4080 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_or:
4081 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_xor:
4082 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_smin:
4083 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_smax:
4084 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_umin:
4085 case Intrinsic::vector_reduce_umax:
4086 FoundReduction = true;
4087 continue;
4088 default:
4089 return false;
4090 }
4091 }
4092
4093 if (!isa<BinaryOperator>(UI) && !isa<ShuffleVectorInst>(UI))
4094 return false;
4095
4096 WorkList.emplace_back(UI);
4097 }
4098 }
4099 return FoundReduction;
4100}
4101
4102/// This method looks for groups of shuffles acting on binops, of the form:
4103/// %x = shuffle ...
4104/// %y = shuffle ...
4105/// %a = binop %x, %y
4106/// %b = binop %x, %y
4107/// shuffle %a, %b, selectmask
4108/// We may, especially if the shuffle is wider than legal, be able to convert
4109/// the shuffle to a form where only parts of a and b need to be computed. On
4110/// architectures with no obvious "select" shuffle, this can reduce the total
4111/// number of operations if the target reports them as cheaper.
4112bool VectorCombine::foldSelectShuffle(Instruction &I, bool FromReduction) {
4113 auto *SVI = cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(&I);
4114 auto *VT = cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
4115 auto *Op0 = dyn_cast<Instruction>(SVI->getOperand(0));
4116 auto *Op1 = dyn_cast<Instruction>(SVI->getOperand(1));
4117 if (!Op0 || !Op1 || Op0 == Op1 || !Op0->isBinaryOp() || !Op1->isBinaryOp() ||
4118 VT != Op0->getType())
4119 return false;
4120
4121 auto *SVI0A = dyn_cast<Instruction>(Op0->getOperand(0));
4122 auto *SVI0B = dyn_cast<Instruction>(Op0->getOperand(1));
4123 auto *SVI1A = dyn_cast<Instruction>(Op1->getOperand(0));
4124 auto *SVI1B = dyn_cast<Instruction>(Op1->getOperand(1));
4125 SmallPtrSet<Instruction *, 4> InputShuffles({SVI0A, SVI0B, SVI1A, SVI1B});
4126 auto checkSVNonOpUses = [&](Instruction *I) {
4127 if (!I || I->getOperand(0)->getType() != VT)
4128 return true;
4129 return any_of(I->users(), [&](User *U) {
4130 return U != Op0 && U != Op1 &&
4131 !(isa<ShuffleVectorInst>(U) &&
4132 (InputShuffles.contains(cast<Instruction>(U)) ||
4133 isInstructionTriviallyDead(cast<Instruction>(U))));
4134 });
4135 };
4136 if (checkSVNonOpUses(SVI0A) || checkSVNonOpUses(SVI0B) ||
4137 checkSVNonOpUses(SVI1A) || checkSVNonOpUses(SVI1B))
4138 return false;
4139
4140 // Collect all the uses that are shuffles that we can transform together. We
4141 // may not have a single shuffle, but a group that can all be transformed
4142 // together profitably.
4143 SmallVector<ShuffleVectorInst *> Shuffles;
4144 auto collectShuffles = [&](Instruction *I) {
4145 for (auto *U : I->users()) {
4146 auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(U);
4147 if (!SV || SV->getType() != VT)
4148 return false;
4149 if ((SV->getOperand(0) != Op0 && SV->getOperand(0) != Op1) ||
4150 (SV->getOperand(1) != Op0 && SV->getOperand(1) != Op1))
4151 return false;
4152 if (!llvm::is_contained(Shuffles, SV))
4153 Shuffles.push_back(SV);
4154 }
4155 return true;
4156 };
4157 if (!collectShuffles(Op0) || !collectShuffles(Op1))
4158 return false;
4159 // From a reduction, we need to be processing a single shuffle, otherwise the
4160 // other uses will not be lane-invariant.
4161 if (FromReduction && Shuffles.size() > 1)
4162 return false;
4163
4164 // Add any shuffle uses for the shuffles we have found, to include them in our
4165 // cost calculations.
4166 if (!FromReduction) {
4167 for (ShuffleVectorInst *SV : Shuffles) {
4168 for (auto *U : SV->users()) {
4169 ShuffleVectorInst *SSV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(U);
4170 if (SSV && isa<UndefValue>(SSV->getOperand(1)) && SSV->getType() == VT)
4171 Shuffles.push_back(SSV);
4172 }
4173 }
4174 }
4175
4176 // For each of the output shuffles, we try to sort all the first vector
4177 // elements to the beginning, followed by the second array elements at the
4178 // end. If the binops are legalized to smaller vectors, this may reduce total
4179 // number of binops. We compute the ReconstructMask mask needed to convert
4180 // back to the original lane order.
4181 SmallVector<std::pair<int, int>> V1, V2;
4182 SmallVector<SmallVector<int>> OrigReconstructMasks;
4183 int MaxV1Elt = 0, MaxV2Elt = 0;
4184 unsigned NumElts = VT->getNumElements();
4185 for (ShuffleVectorInst *SVN : Shuffles) {
4186 SmallVector<int> Mask;
4187 SVN->getShuffleMask(Mask);
4188
4189 // Check the operands are the same as the original, or reversed (in which
4190 // case we need to commute the mask).
4191 Value *SVOp0 = SVN->getOperand(0);
4192 Value *SVOp1 = SVN->getOperand(1);
4193 if (isa<UndefValue>(SVOp1)) {
4194 auto *SSV = cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(SVOp0);
4195 SVOp0 = SSV->getOperand(0);
4196 SVOp1 = SSV->getOperand(1);
4197 for (int &Elem : Mask) {
4198 if (Elem >= static_cast<int>(SSV->getShuffleMask().size()))
4199 return false;
4200 Elem = Elem < 0 ? Elem : SSV->getMaskValue(Elem);
4201 }
4202 }
4203 if (SVOp0 == Op1 && SVOp1 == Op0) {
4204 std::swap(SVOp0, SVOp1);
4205 ShuffleVectorInst::commuteShuffleMask(Mask, NumElts);
4206 }
4207 if (SVOp0 != Op0 || SVOp1 != Op1)
4208 return false;
4209
4210 // Calculate the reconstruction mask for this shuffle, as the mask needed to
4211 // take the packed values from Op0/Op1 and reconstructing to the original
4212 // order.
4213 SmallVector<int> ReconstructMask;
4214 for (unsigned I = 0; I < Mask.size(); I++) {
4215 if (Mask[I] < 0) {
4216 ReconstructMask.push_back(-1);
4217 } else if (Mask[I] < static_cast<int>(NumElts)) {
4218 MaxV1Elt = std::max(MaxV1Elt, Mask[I]);
4219 auto It = find_if(V1, [&](const std::pair<int, int> &A) {
4220 return Mask[I] == A.first;
4221 });
4222 if (It != V1.end())
4223 ReconstructMask.push_back(It - V1.begin());
4224 else {
4225 ReconstructMask.push_back(V1.size());
4226 V1.emplace_back(Mask[I], V1.size());
4227 }
4228 } else {
4229 MaxV2Elt = std::max<int>(MaxV2Elt, Mask[I] - NumElts);
