4230 auto It = find_if(V2, [&](const std::pair<int, int> &A) {
4231 return Mask[I] - static_cast<int>(NumElts) == A.first;
4232 });
4233 if (It != V2.end())
4234 ReconstructMask.push_back(NumElts + It - V2.begin());
4235 else {
4236 ReconstructMask.push_back(NumElts + V2.size());
4237 V2.emplace_back(Mask[I] - NumElts, NumElts + V2.size());
4238 }
4239 }
4240 }
4241
4242 // For reductions, we know that the lane ordering out doesn't alter the
4243 // result. In-order can help simplify the shuffle away.
4244 if (FromReduction)
4245 sort(ReconstructMask);
4246 OrigReconstructMasks.push_back(std::move(ReconstructMask));
4247 }
4248
4249 // If the Maximum element used from V1 and V2 are not larger than the new
4250 // vectors, the vectors are already packes and performing the optimization
4251 // again will likely not help any further. This also prevents us from getting
4252 // stuck in a cycle in case the costs do not also rule it out.
4253 if (V1.empty() || V2.empty() ||
4254 (MaxV1Elt == static_cast<int>(V1.size()) - 1 &&
4255 MaxV2Elt == static_cast<int>(V2.size()) - 1))
4256 return false;
4257
4258 // GetBaseMaskValue takes one of the inputs, which may either be a shuffle, a
4259 // shuffle of another shuffle, or not a shuffle (that is treated like a
4260 // identity shuffle).
4261 auto GetBaseMaskValue = [&](Instruction *I, int M) {
4262 auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(I);
4263 if (!SV)
4264 return M;
4265 if (isa<UndefValue>(SV->getOperand(1)))
4266 if (auto *SSV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(SV->getOperand(0)))
4267 if (InputShuffles.contains(SSV))
4268 return SSV->getMaskValue(SV->getMaskValue(M));
4269 return SV->getMaskValue(M);
4270 };
4271
4272 // Attempt to sort the inputs my ascending mask values to make simpler input
4273 // shuffles and push complex shuffles down to the uses. We sort on the first
4274 // of the two input shuffle orders, to try and get at least one input into a
4275 // nice order.
4276 auto SortBase = [&](Instruction *A, std::pair<int, int> X,
4277 std::pair<int, int> Y) {
4278 int MXA = GetBaseMaskValue(A, X.first);
4279 int MYA = GetBaseMaskValue(A, Y.first);
4280 return MXA < MYA;
4281 };
4282 stable_sort(V1, [&](std::pair<int, int> A, std::pair<int, int> B) {
4283 return SortBase(SVI0A, A, B);
4284 });
4285 stable_sort(V2, [&](std::pair<int, int> A, std::pair<int, int> B) {
4286 return SortBase(SVI1A, A, B);
4287 });
4288 // Calculate our ReconstructMasks from the OrigReconstructMasks and the
4289 // modified order of the input shuffles.
4290 SmallVector<SmallVector<int>> ReconstructMasks;
4291 for (const auto &Mask : OrigReconstructMasks) {
4292 SmallVector<int> ReconstructMask;
4293 for (int M : Mask) {
4294 auto FindIndex = [](const SmallVector<std::pair<int, int>> &V, int M) {
4295 auto It = find_if(V, [M](auto A) { return A.second == M; });
4296 assert(It != V.end() && "Expected all entries in Mask");
4297 return std::distance(V.begin(), It);
4298 };
4299 if (M < 0)
4300 ReconstructMask.push_back(-1);
4301 else if (M < static_cast<int>(NumElts)) {
4302 ReconstructMask.push_back(FindIndex(V1, M));
4303 } else {
4304 ReconstructMask.push_back(NumElts + FindIndex(V2, M));
4305 }
4306 }
4307 ReconstructMasks.push_back(std::move(ReconstructMask));
4308 }
4309
4310 // Calculate the masks needed for the new input shuffles, which get padded
4311 // with undef
4312 SmallVector<int> V1A, V1B, V2A, V2B;
4313 for (unsigned I = 0; I < V1.size(); I++) {
4314 V1A.push_back(GetBaseMaskValue(SVI0A, V1[I].first));
4315 V1B.push_back(GetBaseMaskValue(SVI0B, V1[I].first));
4316 }
4317 for (unsigned I = 0; I < V2.size(); I++) {
4318 V2A.push_back(GetBaseMaskValue(SVI1A, V2[I].first));
4319 V2B.push_back(GetBaseMaskValue(SVI1B, V2[I].first));
4320 }
4321 while (V1A.size() < NumElts) {
4322 V1A.push_back(PoisonMaskElem);
4323 V1B.push_back(PoisonMaskElem);
4324 }
4325 while (V2A.size() < NumElts) {
4326 V2A.push_back(PoisonMaskElem);
4327 V2B.push_back(PoisonMaskElem);
4328 }
4329
4330 auto AddShuffleCost = [&](InstructionCost C, Instruction *I) {
4331 auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(I);
4332 if (!SV)
4333 return C;
4334 return C + TTI.getShuffleCost(isa<UndefValue>(SV->getOperand(1))
4335 ? TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc
4336 : TTI::SK_PermuteTwoSrc,
4337 VT, VT, SV->getShuffleMask(), CostKind);
4338 };
4339 auto AddShuffleMaskCost = [&](InstructionCost C, ArrayRef<int> Mask) {
4340 return C +
4341 TTI.getShuffleCost(TTI::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, VT, VT, Mask, CostKind);
4342 };
4343
4344 unsigned ElementSize = VT->getElementType()->getPrimitiveSizeInBits();
4345 unsigned MaxVectorSize =
4346 TTI.getRegisterBitWidth(TargetTransformInfo::RGK_FixedWidthVector);
4347 unsigned MaxElementsInVector = MaxVectorSize / ElementSize;
4348 if (MaxElementsInVector == 0)
4349 return false;
4350 // When there are multiple shufflevector operations on the same input,
4351 // especially when the vector length is larger than the register size,
4352 // identical shuffle patterns may occur across different groups of elements.
4353 // To avoid overestimating the cost by counting these repeated shuffles more
4354 // than once, we only account for unique shuffle patterns. This adjustment
4355 // prevents inflated costs in the cost model for wide vectors split into
4356 // several register-sized groups.
4357 std::set<SmallVector<int, 4>> UniqueShuffles;
4358 auto AddShuffleMaskAdjustedCost = [&](InstructionCost C, ArrayRef<int> Mask) {
4359 // Compute the cost for performing the shuffle over the full vector.
4360 auto ShuffleCost =
4361 TTI.getShuffleCost(TTI::SK_PermuteTwoSrc, VT, VT, Mask, CostKind);
4362 unsigned NumFullVectors = Mask.size() / MaxElementsInVector;
4363 if (NumFullVectors < 2)
4364 return C + ShuffleCost;
4365 SmallVector<int, 4> SubShuffle(MaxElementsInVector);
4366 unsigned NumUniqueGroups = 0;
4367 unsigned NumGroups = Mask.size() / MaxElementsInVector;
4368 // For each group of MaxElementsInVector contiguous elements,
4369 // collect their shuffle pattern and insert into the set of unique patterns.
4370 for (unsigned I = 0; I < NumFullVectors; ++I) {
4371 for (unsigned J = 0; J < MaxElementsInVector; ++J)
4372 SubShuffle[J] = Mask[MaxElementsInVector * I + J];
4373 if (UniqueShuffles.insert(SubShuffle).second)
4374 NumUniqueGroups += 1;
4375 }
4376 return C + ShuffleCost * NumUniqueGroups / NumGroups;
4377 };
4378 auto AddShuffleAdjustedCost = [&](InstructionCost C, Instruction *I) {
4379 auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(I);
4380 if (!SV)
4381 return C;
4382 SmallVector<int, 16> Mask;
4383 SV->getShuffleMask(Mask);
4384 return AddShuffleMaskAdjustedCost(C, Mask);
4385 };
4386 // Check that input consists of ShuffleVectors applied to the same input
4387 auto AllShufflesHaveSameOperands =
4388 [](SmallPtrSetImpl<Instruction *> &InputShuffles) {
4389 if (InputShuffles.size() < 2)
4390 return false;
4391 ShuffleVectorInst *FirstSV =
4392 dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(*InputShuffles.begin());
4393 if (!FirstSV)
4394 return false;
4395
4396 Value *In0 = FirstSV->getOperand(0), *In1 = FirstSV->getOperand(1);
4397 return std::all_of(
4398 std::next(InputShuffles.begin()), InputShuffles.end(),
4399 [&](Instruction *I) {
4400 ShuffleVectorInst *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(I);
4401 return SV && SV->getOperand(0) == In0 && SV->getOperand(1) == In1;
4402 });
4403 };
4404
4405 // Get the costs of the shuffles + binops before and after with the new
4406 // shuffle masks.
4407 InstructionCost CostBefore =
4408 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Op0->getOpcode(), VT, CostKind) +
4409 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Op1->getOpcode(), VT, CostKind);
4410 CostBefore += std::accumulate(Shuffles.begin(), Shuffles.end(),
4411 InstructionCost(0), AddShuffleCost);
4412 if (AllShufflesHaveSameOperands(InputShuffles)) {
4413 UniqueShuffles.clear();
4414 CostBefore += std::accumulate(InputShuffles.begin(), InputShuffles.end(),
4415 InstructionCost(0), AddShuffleAdjustedCost);
4416 } else {
4417 CostBefore += std::accumulate(InputShuffles.begin(), InputShuffles.end(),
4418 InstructionCost(0), AddShuffleCost);
4419 }
4420
4421 // The new binops will be unused for lanes past the used shuffle lengths.
4422 // These types attempt to get the correct cost for that from the target.
4423 FixedVectorType *Op0SmallVT =
4424 FixedVectorType::get(VT->getScalarType(), V1.size());
4425 FixedVectorType *Op1SmallVT =
4426 FixedVectorType::get(VT->getScalarType(), V2.size());
4427 InstructionCost CostAfter =
4428 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Op0->getOpcode(), Op0SmallVT, CostKind) +
4429 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(Op1->getOpcode(), Op1SmallVT, CostKind);
4430 UniqueShuffles.clear();
4431 CostAfter += std::accumulate(ReconstructMasks.begin(), ReconstructMasks.end(),
4432 InstructionCost(0), AddShuffleMaskAdjustedCost);
4433 std::set<SmallVector<int>> OutputShuffleMasks({V1A, V1B, V2A, V2B});
4434 CostAfter +=
4435 std::accumulate(OutputShuffleMasks.begin(), OutputShuffleMasks.end(),
4436 InstructionCost(0), AddShuffleMaskCost);
4437
4438 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a binop select shuffle pattern: " << I << "\n");
4439 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << " CostBefore: " << CostBefore
4440 << " vs CostAfter: " << CostAfter << "\n");
4441 if (CostBefore < CostAfter ||
4442 (CostBefore == CostAfter && !feedsIntoVectorReduction(SVI)))
4443 return false;
4444
4445 // The cost model has passed, create the new instructions.
4446 auto GetShuffleOperand = [&](Instruction *I, unsigned Op) -> Value * {
4447 auto *SV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(I);
4448 if (!SV)
4449 return I;
4450 if (isa<UndefValue>(SV->getOperand(1)))
4451 if (auto *SSV = dyn_cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(SV->getOperand(0)))
4452 if (InputShuffles.contains(SSV))
4453 return SSV->getOperand(Op);
4454 return SV->getOperand(Op);
4455 };
4456 Builder.SetInsertPoint(*SVI0A->getInsertionPointAfterDef());
4457 Value *NSV0A = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(GetShuffleOperand(SVI0A, 0),
4458 GetShuffleOperand(SVI0A, 1), V1A);
4459 Builder.SetInsertPoint(*SVI0B->getInsertionPointAfterDef());
4460 Value *NSV0B = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(GetShuffleOperand(SVI0B, 0),
4461 GetShuffleOperand(SVI0B, 1), V1B);
4462 Builder.SetInsertPoint(*SVI1A->getInsertionPointAfterDef());
4463 Value *NSV1A = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(GetShuffleOperand(SVI1A, 0),
4464 GetShuffleOperand(SVI1A, 1), V2A);
4465 Builder.SetInsertPoint(*SVI1B->getInsertionPointAfterDef());
4466 Value *NSV1B = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(GetShuffleOperand(SVI1B, 0),
4467 GetShuffleOperand(SVI1B, 1), V2B);
4468 Builder.SetInsertPoint(Op0);
4469 Value *NOp0 = Builder.CreateBinOp((Instruction::BinaryOps)Op0->getOpcode(),
4470 NSV0A, NSV0B);
4471 if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(NOp0))
4472 I->copyIRFlags(Op0, true);
4473 Builder.SetInsertPoint(Op1);
4474 Value *NOp1 = Builder.CreateBinOp((Instruction::BinaryOps)Op1->getOpcode(),
4475 NSV1A, NSV1B);
4476 if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(NOp1))
4477 I->copyIRFlags(Op1, true);
4478
4479 for (int S = 0, E = ReconstructMasks.size(); S != E; S++) {
4480 Builder.SetInsertPoint(Shuffles[S]);
4481 Value *NSV = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(NOp0, NOp1, ReconstructMasks[S]);
4482 replaceValue(*Shuffles[S], *NSV, false);
4483 }
4484
4485 Worklist.pushValue(NSV0A);
4486 Worklist.pushValue(NSV0B);
4487 Worklist.pushValue(NSV1A);
4488 Worklist.pushValue(NSV1B);
4489 return true;
4490}
4491
4492/// Check if instruction depends on ZExt and this ZExt can be moved after the
4493/// instruction. Move ZExt if it is profitable. For example:
4494/// logic(zext(x),y) -> zext(logic(x,trunc(y)))
4495/// lshr((zext(x),y) -> zext(lshr(x,trunc(y)))
4496/// Cost model calculations takes into account if zext(x) has other users and
4497/// whether it can be propagated through them too.
4498bool VectorCombine::shrinkType(Instruction &I) {
4499 Value *ZExted, *OtherOperand;
4500 if (!match(&I, m_c_BitwiseLogic(m_ZExt(m_Value(ZExted)),
4501 m_Value(OtherOperand))) &&
4502 !match(&I, m_LShr(m_ZExt(m_Value(ZExted)), m_Value(OtherOperand))))
4503 return false;
4504
4505 Value *ZExtOperand = I.getOperand(I.getOperand(0) == OtherOperand ? 1 : 0);
4506
4507 auto *BigTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
4508 auto *SmallTy = cast<FixedVectorType>(ZExted->getType());
4509 unsigned BW = SmallTy->getElementType()->getPrimitiveSizeInBits();
4510
4511 if (I.getOpcode() == Instruction::LShr) {
4512 // Check that the shift amount is less than the number of bits in the
4513 // smaller type. Otherwise, the smaller lshr will return a poison value.
4514 KnownBits ShAmtKB = computeKnownBits(I.getOperand(1), *DL);
4515 if (ShAmtKB.getMaxValue().uge(BW))
4516 return false;
4517 } else {
4518 // Check that the expression overall uses at most the same number of bits as
4519 // ZExted
4520 KnownBits KB = computeKnownBits(&I, *DL);
4521 if (KB.countMaxActiveBits() > BW)
4522 return false;
4523 }
4524
4525 // Calculate costs of leaving current IR as it is and moving ZExt operation
4526 // later, along with adding truncates if needed
4527 InstructionCost ZExtCost = TTI.getCastInstrCost(
4528 Instruction::ZExt, BigTy, SmallTy,
4529 TargetTransformInfo::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
4530 InstructionCost CurrentCost = ZExtCost;
4531 InstructionCost ShrinkCost = 0;
4532
4533 // Calculate total cost and check that we can propagate through all ZExt users
4534 for (User *U : ZExtOperand->users()) {
4535 auto *UI = cast<Instruction>(U);
4536 if (UI == &I) {
4537 CurrentCost +=
4538 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(UI->getOpcode(), BigTy, CostKind);
4539 ShrinkCost +=
4540 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(UI->getOpcode(), SmallTy, CostKind);
4541 ShrinkCost += ZExtCost;
4542 continue;
4543 }
4544
4545 if (!Instruction::isBinaryOp(UI->getOpcode()))
4546 return false;
4547
4548 // Check if we can propagate ZExt through its other users
4549 KnownBits KB = computeKnownBits(UI, *DL);
4550 if (KB.countMaxActiveBits() > BW)
4551 return false;
4552
4553 CurrentCost += TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(UI->getOpcode(), BigTy, CostKind);
4554 ShrinkCost +=
4555 TTI.getArithmeticInstrCost(UI->getOpcode(), SmallTy, CostKind);
4556 ShrinkCost += ZExtCost;
4557 }
4558
4559 // If the other instruction operand is not a constant, we'll need to
4560 // generate a truncate instruction. So we have to adjust cost
4561 if (!isa<Constant>(OtherOperand))
4562 ShrinkCost += TTI.getCastInstrCost(
4563 Instruction::Trunc, SmallTy, BigTy,
4564 TargetTransformInfo::CastContextHint::None, CostKind);
4565
4566 // If the cost of shrinking types and leaving the IR is the same, we'll lean
4567 // towards modifying the IR because shrinking opens opportunities for other
4568 // shrinking optimisations.
4569 if (ShrinkCost > CurrentCost)
4570 return false;
4571
4572 Builder.SetInsertPoint(&I);
4573 Value *Op0 = ZExted;
4574 Value *Op1 = Builder.CreateTrunc(OtherOperand, SmallTy);
4575 // Keep the order of operands the same
4576 if (I.getOperand(0) == OtherOperand)
4577 std::swap(Op0, Op1);
4578 Value *NewBinOp =
4579 Builder.CreateBinOp((Instruction::BinaryOps)I.getOpcode(), Op0, Op1);
4580 cast<Instruction>(NewBinOp)->copyIRFlags(&I);
4581 cast<Instruction>(NewBinOp)->copyMetadata(I);
4582 Value *NewZExtr = Builder.CreateZExt(NewBinOp, BigTy);
4583 replaceValue(I, *NewZExtr);
4584 return true;
4585}
4586
4587/// insert (DstVec, (extract SrcVec, ExtIdx), InsIdx) -->
4588/// shuffle (DstVec, SrcVec, Mask)
4589bool VectorCombine::foldInsExtVectorToShuffle(Instruction &I) {
4590 Value *DstVec, *SrcVec;
4591 uint64_t ExtIdx, InsIdx;
4592 if (!match(&I,
4593 m_InsertElt(m_Value(DstVec),
4594 m_ExtractElt(m_Value(SrcVec), m_ConstantInt(ExtIdx)),
4595 m_ConstantInt(InsIdx))))
4596 return false;
4597
4598 auto *DstVecTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
4599 auto *SrcVecTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(SrcVec->getType());
4600 // We can try combining vectors with different element sizes.
4601 if (!DstVecTy || !SrcVecTy ||
4602 SrcVecTy->getElementType() != DstVecTy->getElementType())
4603 return false;
4604
4605 unsigned NumDstElts = DstVecTy->getNumElements();
4606 unsigned NumSrcElts = SrcVecTy->getNumElements();
4607 if (InsIdx >= NumDstElts || ExtIdx >= NumSrcElts || NumDstElts == 1)
4608 return false;
4609
4610 // Insertion into poison is a cheaper single operand shuffle.
4611 TargetTransformInfo::ShuffleKind SK;
4612 SmallVector<int> Mask(NumDstElts, PoisonMaskElem);
4613
4614 bool NeedExpOrNarrow = NumSrcElts != NumDstElts;
4615 bool NeedDstSrcSwap = isa<PoisonValue>(DstVec) && !isa<UndefValue>(SrcVec);
4616 if (NeedDstSrcSwap) {
4617 SK = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc;
4618 Mask[InsIdx] = ExtIdx % NumDstElts;
4619 std::swap(DstVec, SrcVec);
4620 } else {
4621 SK = TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteTwoSrc;
4622 std::iota(Mask.begin(), Mask.end(), 0);
4623 Mask[InsIdx] = (ExtIdx % NumDstElts) + NumDstElts;
4624 }
4625
4626 // Cost
4627 auto *Ins = cast<InsertElementInst>(&I);
4628 auto *Ext = cast<ExtractElementInst>(I.getOperand(1));
4629 InstructionCost InsCost =
4630 TTI.getVectorInstrCost(*Ins, DstVecTy, CostKind, InsIdx);
4631 InstructionCost ExtCost =
4632 TTI.getVectorInstrCost(*Ext, DstVecTy, CostKind, ExtIdx);
4633 InstructionCost OldCost = ExtCost + InsCost;
4634
4635 InstructionCost NewCost = 0;
4636 SmallVector<int> ExtToVecMask;
4637 if (!NeedExpOrNarrow) {
4638 // Ignore 'free' identity insertion shuffle.
4639 // TODO: getShuffleCost should return TCC_Free for Identity shuffles.
4640 if (!ShuffleVectorInst::isIdentityMask(Mask, NumSrcElts))
4641 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(SK, DstVecTy, DstVecTy, Mask, CostKind, 0,
4642 nullptr, {DstVec, SrcVec});
4643 } else {
4644 // When creating a length-changing-vector, always try to keep the relevant
4645 // element in an equivalent position, so that bulk shuffles are more likely
4646 // to be useful.
4647 ExtToVecMask.assign(NumDstElts, PoisonMaskElem);
4648 ExtToVecMask[ExtIdx % NumDstElts] = ExtIdx;
4649 // Add cost for expanding or narrowing
4650 NewCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(TargetTransformInfo::SK_PermuteSingleSrc,
4651 DstVecTy, SrcVecTy, ExtToVecMask, CostKind);
4652 NewCost += TTI.getShuffleCost(SK, DstVecTy, DstVecTy, Mask, CostKind);
4653 }
4654
4655 if (!Ext->hasOneUse())
4656 NewCost += ExtCost;
4657
4658 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a insert/extract shuffle-like pair: " << I
4659 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
4660 << "\n");
4661
4662 if (OldCost < NewCost)
4663 return false;
4664
4665 if (NeedExpOrNarrow) {
4666 if (!NeedDstSrcSwap)
4667 SrcVec = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(SrcVec, ExtToVecMask);
4668 else
4669 DstVec = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(DstVec, ExtToVecMask);
4670 }
4671
4672 // Canonicalize undef param to RHS to help further folds.
4673 if (isa<UndefValue>(DstVec) && !isa<UndefValue>(SrcVec)) {
4674 ShuffleVectorInst::commuteShuffleMask(Mask, NumDstElts);
4675 std::swap(DstVec, SrcVec);
4676 }
4677
4678 Value *Shuf = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(DstVec, SrcVec, Mask);
4679 replaceValue(I, *Shuf);
4680
4681 return true;
4682}
4683
4684/// If we're interleaving 2 constant splats, for instance `<vscale x 8 x i32>
4685/// <splat of 666>` and `<vscale x 8 x i32> <splat of 777>`, we can create a
4686/// larger splat `<vscale x 8 x i64> <splat of ((777 << 32) | 666)>` first
4687/// before casting it back into `<vscale x 16 x i32>`.
4688bool VectorCombine::foldInterleaveIntrinsics(Instruction &I) {
4689 const APInt *SplatVal0, *SplatVal1;
4690 if (!match(&I, m_Intrinsic<Intrinsic::vector_interleave2>(
4691 m_APInt(SplatVal0), m_APInt(SplatVal1))))
4692 return false;
4693
4694 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "VC: Folding interleave2 with two splats: " << I
4695 << "\n");
4696
4697 auto *VTy =
4698 cast<VectorType>(cast<IntrinsicInst>(I).getArgOperand(0)->getType());
4699 auto *ExtVTy = VectorType::getExtendedElementVectorType(VTy);
4700 unsigned Width = VTy->getElementType()->getIntegerBitWidth();
4701
4702 // Just in case the cost of interleave2 intrinsic and bitcast are both
4703 // invalid, in which case we want to bail out, we use <= rather
4704 // than < here. Even they both have valid and equal costs, it's probably
4705 // not a good idea to emit a high-cost constant splat.
4706 if (TTI.getInstructionCost(&I, CostKind) <=
4707 TTI.getCastInstrCost(Instruction::BitCast, I.getType(), ExtVTy,
4708 TTI::CastContextHint::None, CostKind)) {
4709 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "VC: The cost to cast from " << *ExtVTy << " to "
4710 << *I.getType() << " is too high.\n");
4711 return false;
4712 }
4713
4714 APInt NewSplatVal = SplatVal1->zext(Width * 2);
4715 NewSplatVal <<= Width;
4716 NewSplatVal |= SplatVal0->zext(Width * 2);
4717 auto *NewSplat = ConstantVector::getSplat(
4718 ExtVTy->getElementCount(), ConstantInt::get(F.getContext(), NewSplatVal));
4719
4720 IRBuilder<> Builder(&I);
4721 replaceValue(I, *Builder.CreateBitCast(NewSplat, I.getType()));
4722 return true;
4723}
4724
4725// Attempt to shrink loads that are only used by shufflevector instructions.
4726bool VectorCombine::shrinkLoadForShuffles(Instruction &I) {
4727 auto *OldLoad = dyn_cast<LoadInst>(&I);
4728 if (!OldLoad || !OldLoad->isSimple())
4729 return false;
4730
4731 auto *OldLoadTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(OldLoad->getType());
4732 if (!OldLoadTy)
4733 return false;
4734
4735 unsigned const OldNumElements = OldLoadTy->getNumElements();
4736
4737 // Search all uses of load. If all uses are shufflevector instructions, and
4738 // the second operands are all poison values, find the minimum and maximum
4739 // indices of the vector elements referenced by all shuffle masks.
4740 // Otherwise return `std::nullopt`.
4741 using IndexRange = std::pair<int, int>;
4742 auto GetIndexRangeInShuffles = [&]() -> std::optional<IndexRange> {
4743 IndexRange OutputRange = IndexRange(OldNumElements, -1);
4744 for (llvm::Use &Use : I.uses()) {
4745 // Ensure all uses match the required pattern.
4746 User *Shuffle = Use.getUser();
4747 ArrayRef<int> Mask;
4748
4749 if (!match(Shuffle,
4750 m_Shuffle(m_Specific(OldLoad), m_Undef(), m_Mask(Mask))))
4751 return std::nullopt;
4752
4753 // Ignore shufflevector instructions that have no uses.
4754 if (Shuffle->use_empty())
4755 continue;
4756
4757 // Find the min and max indices used by the shufflevector instruction.
4758 for (int Index : Mask) {
4759 if (Index >= 0 && Index < static_cast<int>(OldNumElements)) {
4760 OutputRange.first = std::min(Index, OutputRange.first);
4761 OutputRange.second = std::max(Index, OutputRange.second);
4762 }
4763 }
4764 }
4765
4766 if (OutputRange.second < OutputRange.first)
4767 return std::nullopt;
4768
4769 return OutputRange;
4770 };
4771
4772 // Get the range of vector elements used by shufflevector instructions.
4773 if (std::optional<IndexRange> Indices = GetIndexRangeInShuffles()) {
4774 unsigned const NewNumElements = Indices->second + 1u;
4775
4776 // If the range of vector elements is smaller than the full load, attempt
4777 // to create a smaller load.
4778 if (NewNumElements < OldNumElements) {
4779 IRBuilder Builder(&I);
4780 Builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(I.getDebugLoc());
4781
4782 // Calculate costs of old and new ops.
4783 Type *ElemTy = OldLoadTy->getElementType();
4784 FixedVectorType *NewLoadTy = FixedVectorType::get(ElemTy, NewNumElements);
4785 Value *PtrOp = OldLoad->getPointerOperand();
4786
4787 InstructionCost OldCost = TTI.getMemoryOpCost(
4788 Instruction::Load, OldLoad->getType(), OldLoad->getAlign(),
4789 OldLoad->getPointerAddressSpace(), CostKind);
4790 InstructionCost NewCost =
4791 TTI.getMemoryOpCost(Instruction::Load, NewLoadTy, OldLoad->getAlign(),
4792 OldLoad->getPointerAddressSpace(), CostKind);
4793
4794 using UseEntry = std::pair<ShuffleVectorInst *, std::vector<int>>;
4795 SmallVector<UseEntry, 4u> NewUses;
4796 unsigned const MaxIndex = NewNumElements * 2u;
4797
4798 for (llvm::Use &Use : I.uses()) {
4799 auto *Shuffle = cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(Use.getUser());
4800 ArrayRef<int> OldMask = Shuffle->getShuffleMask();
4801
4802 // Create entry for new use.
4803 NewUses.push_back({Shuffle, OldMask});
4804
4805 // Validate mask indices.
4806 for (int Index : OldMask) {
4807 if (Index >= static_cast<int>(MaxIndex))
4808 return false;
4809 }
4810
4811 // Update costs.
4812 OldCost +=
4813 TTI.getShuffleCost(TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc, Shuffle->getType(),
4814 OldLoadTy, OldMask, CostKind);
4815 NewCost +=
4816 TTI.getShuffleCost(TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc, Shuffle->getType(),
4817 NewLoadTy, OldMask, CostKind);
4818 }
4819
4820 LLVM_DEBUG(
4821 dbgs() << "Found a load used only by shufflevector instructions: "
4822 << I << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost
4823 << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost << "\n");
4824
4825 if (OldCost < NewCost || !NewCost.isValid())
4826 return false;
4827
4828 // Create new load of smaller vector.
4829 auto *NewLoad = cast<LoadInst>(
4830 Builder.CreateAlignedLoad(NewLoadTy, PtrOp, OldLoad->getAlign()));
4831 NewLoad->copyMetadata(I);
4832
4833 // Replace all uses.
4834 for (UseEntry &Use : NewUses) {
4835 ShuffleVectorInst *Shuffle = Use.first;
4836 std::vector<int> &NewMask = Use.second;
4837
4838 Builder.SetInsertPoint(Shuffle);
4839 Builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(Shuffle->getDebugLoc());
4840 Value *NewShuffle = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(
4841 NewLoad, PoisonValue::get(NewLoadTy), NewMask);
4842
4843 replaceValue(*Shuffle, *NewShuffle, false);
4844 }
4845
4846 return true;
4847 }
4848 }
4849 return false;
4850}
4851
4852// Attempt to narrow a phi of shufflevector instructions where the two incoming
4853// values have the same operands but different masks. If the two shuffle masks
4854// are offsets of one another we can use one branch to rotate the incoming
4855// vector and perform one larger shuffle after the phi.
4856bool VectorCombine::shrinkPhiOfShuffles(Instruction &I) {
4857 auto *Phi = dyn_cast<PHINode>(&I);
4858 if (!Phi || Phi->getNumIncomingValues() != 2u)
4859 return false;
4860
4861 Value *Op = nullptr;
4862 ArrayRef<int> Mask0;
4863 ArrayRef<int> Mask1;
4864
4865 if (!match(Phi->getOperand(0u),
4866 m_OneUse(m_Shuffle(m_Value(Op), m_Poison(), m_Mask(Mask0)))) ||
4867 !match(Phi->getOperand(1u),
4868 m_OneUse(m_Shuffle(m_Specific(Op), m_Poison(), m_Mask(Mask1)))))
4869 return false;
4870
4871 auto *Shuf = cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(Phi->getOperand(0u));
4872
4873 // Ensure result vectors are wider than the argument vector.
4874 auto *InputVT = cast<FixedVectorType>(Op->getType());
4875 auto *ResultVT = cast<FixedVectorType>(Shuf->getType());
4876 auto const InputNumElements = InputVT->getNumElements();
4877
4878 if (InputNumElements >= ResultVT->getNumElements())
4879 return false;
4880
4881 // Take the difference of the two shuffle masks at each index. Ignore poison
4882 // values at the same index in both masks.
4883 SmallVector<int, 16> NewMask;
4884 NewMask.reserve(Mask0.size());
4885
4886 for (auto [M0, M1] : zip(Mask0, Mask1)) {
4887 if (M0 >= 0 && M1 >= 0)
4888 NewMask.push_back(M0 - M1);
4889 else if (M0 == -1 && M1 == -1)
4890 continue;
4891 else
4892 return false;
4893 }
4894
4895 // Ensure all elements of the new mask are equal. If the difference between
4896 // the incoming mask elements is the same, the two must be constant offsets
4897 // of one another.
4898 if (NewMask.empty() || !all_equal(NewMask))
4899 return false;
4900
4901 // Create new mask using difference of the two incoming masks.
4902 int MaskOffset = NewMask[0u];
4903 unsigned Index = (InputNumElements + MaskOffset) % InputNumElements;
4904 NewMask.clear();
4905
4906 for (unsigned I = 0u; I < InputNumElements; ++I) {
4907 NewMask.push_back(Index);
4908 Index = (Index + 1u) % InputNumElements;
4909 }
4910
4911 // Calculate costs for worst cases and compare.
4912 auto const Kind = TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc;
4913 auto OldCost =
4914 std::max(TTI.getShuffleCost(Kind, ResultVT, InputVT, Mask0, CostKind),
4915 TTI.getShuffleCost(Kind, ResultVT, InputVT, Mask1, CostKind));
4916 auto NewCost = TTI.getShuffleCost(Kind, InputVT, InputVT, NewMask, CostKind) +
4917 TTI.getShuffleCost(Kind, ResultVT, InputVT, Mask1, CostKind);
4918
4919 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "Found a phi of mergeable shuffles: " << I
4920 << "\n OldCost: " << OldCost << " vs NewCost: " << NewCost
4921 << "\n");
4922
4923 if (NewCost > OldCost)
4924 return false;
4925
4926 // Create new shuffles and narrowed phi.
4927 auto Builder = IRBuilder(Shuf);
4928 Builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(Shuf->getDebugLoc());
4929 auto *PoisonVal = PoisonValue::get(InputVT);
4930 auto *NewShuf0 = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Op, PoisonVal, NewMask);
4931 Worklist.push(cast<Instruction>(NewShuf0));
4932
4933 Builder.SetInsertPoint(Phi);
4934 Builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(Phi->getDebugLoc());
4935 auto *NewPhi = Builder.CreatePHI(NewShuf0->getType(), 2u);
4936 NewPhi->addIncoming(NewShuf0, Phi->getIncomingBlock(0u));
4937 NewPhi->addIncoming(Op, Phi->getIncomingBlock(1u));
4938
4939 Builder.SetInsertPoint(*NewPhi->getInsertionPointAfterDef());
4940 PoisonVal = PoisonValue::get(NewPhi->getType());
4941 auto *NewShuf1 = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(NewPhi, PoisonVal, Mask1);
4942
4943 replaceValue(*Phi, *NewShuf1);
4944 return true;
4945}
4946
4947/// This is the entry point for all transforms. Pass manager differences are
4948/// handled in the callers of this function.
4949bool VectorCombine::run() {
4950 if (DisableVectorCombine)
4951 return false;
4952
4953 // Don't attempt vectorization if the target does not support vectors.
4954 if (!TTI.getNumberOfRegisters(TTI.getRegisterClassForType(/*Vector*/ true)))
4955 return false;
4956
4957 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "\n\nVECTORCOMBINE on " << F.getName() << "\n");
4958
4959 auto FoldInst = [this](Instruction &I) {
4960 Builder.SetInsertPoint(&I);
4961 bool IsVectorType = isa<VectorType>(I.getType());
4962 bool IsFixedVectorType = isa<FixedVectorType>(I.getType());
4963 auto Opcode = I.getOpcode();
4964
4965 LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "VC: Visiting: " << I << '\n');
4966
4967 // These folds should be beneficial regardless of when this pass is run
4968 // in the optimization pipeline.
4969 // The type checking is for run-time efficiency. We can avoid wasting time
4970 // dispatching to folding functions if there's no chance of matching.
4971 if (IsFixedVectorType) {
4972 switch (Opcode) {
4973 case Instruction::InsertElement:
4974 if (vectorizeLoadInsert(I))
4975 return true;
4976 break;
4977 case Instruction::ShuffleVector:
4978 if (widenSubvectorLoad(I))
4979 return true;
4980 break;
4981 default:
4982 break;
4983 }
4984 }
4985
4986 // This transform works with scalable and fixed vectors
4987 // TODO: Identify and allow other scalable transforms
4988 if (IsVectorType) {
4989 if (scalarizeOpOrCmp(I))
4990 return true;
4991 if (scalarizeLoad(I))
4992 return true;
4993 if (scalarizeExtExtract(I))
4994 return true;
4995 if (scalarizeVPIntrinsic(I))
4996 return true;
4997 if (foldInterleaveIntrinsics(I))
4998 return true;
4999 }
5000
5001 if (Opcode == Instruction::Store)
5002 if (foldSingleElementStore(I))
5003 return true;
5004
5005 // If this is an early pipeline invocation of this pass, we are done.
5006 if (TryEarlyFoldsOnly)
5007 return false;
5008
5009 // Otherwise, try folds that improve codegen but may interfere with
5010 // early IR canonicalizations.
5011 // The type checking is for run-time efficiency. We can avoid wasting time
5012 // dispatching to folding functions if there's no chance of matching.
5013 if (IsFixedVectorType) {
5014 switch (Opcode) {
5015 case Instruction::InsertElement:
5016 if (foldInsExtFNeg(I))
5017 return true;
5018 if (foldInsExtBinop(I))
5019 return true;
5020 if (foldInsExtVectorToShuffle(I))
5021 return true;
5022 break;
5023 case Instruction::ShuffleVector:
5024 if (foldPermuteOfBinops(I))
5025 return true;
5026 if (foldShuffleOfBinops(I))
5027 return true;
5028 if (foldShuffleOfSelects(I))
5029 return true;
5030 if (foldShuffleOfCastops(I))
5031 return true;
5032 if (foldShuffleOfShuffles(I))
5033 return true;
5034 if (foldPermuteOfIntrinsic(I))
5035 return true;
5036 if (foldShufflesOfLengthChangingShuffles(I))
5037 return true;
5038 if (foldShuffleOfIntrinsics(I))
5039 return true;
5040 if (foldSelectShuffle(I))
5041 return true;
5042 if (foldShuffleToIdentity(I))
5043 return true;
5044 break;
5045 case Instruction::Load:
5046 if (shrinkLoadForShuffles(I))
5047 return true;
5048 break;
5049 case Instruction::BitCast:
5050 if (foldBitcastShuffle(I))
5051 return true;
5052 break;
5053 case Instruction::And:
5054 case Instruction::Or:
5055 case Instruction::Xor:
5056 if (foldBitOpOfCastops(I))
5057 return true;
5058 if (foldBitOpOfCastConstant(I))
5059 return true;
5060 break;
5061 case Instruction::PHI:
5062 if (shrinkPhiOfShuffles(I))
5063 return true;
5064 break;
5065 default:
5066 if (shrinkType(I))
5067 return true;
5068 break;
5069 }
5070 } else {
5071 switch (Opcode) {
5072 case Instruction::Call:
5073 if (foldShuffleFromReductions(I))
5074 return true;
5075 if (foldCastFromReductions(I))
5076 return true;
5077 break;
5078 case Instruction::ExtractElement:
5079 if (foldShuffleChainsToReduce(I))
5080 return true;
5081 break;
5082 case Instruction::ICmp:
5083 case Instruction::FCmp:
5084 if (foldExtractExtract(I))
5085 return true;
5086 break;
5087 case Instruction::Or:
5088 if (foldConcatOfBoolMasks(I))
5089 return true;
5090 [[fallthrough]];
5091 default:
5092 if (Instruction::isBinaryOp(Opcode)) {
5093 if (foldExtractExtract(I))
5094 return true;
5095 if (foldExtractedCmps(I))
5096 return true;
5097 if (foldBinopOfReductions(I))
5098 return true;
5099 }
5100 break;
5101 }
5102 }
5103 return false;
5104 };
5105
5106 bool MadeChange = false;
5107 for (BasicBlock &BB : F) {
5108 // Ignore unreachable basic blocks.
5109 if (!DT.isReachableFromEntry(&BB))
5110 continue;
5111 // Use early increment range so that we can erase instructions in loop.
5112 // make_early_inc_range is not applicable here, as the next iterator may
5113 // be invalidated by RecursivelyDeleteTriviallyDeadInstructions.
5114 // We manually maintain the next instruction and update it when it is about
5115 // to be deleted.
5116 Instruction *I = &BB.front();
5117 while (I) {
5118 NextInst = I->getNextNode();
5119 if (!I->isDebugOrPseudoInst())
5120 MadeChange |= FoldInst(*I);
5121 I = NextInst;
5122 }
5123 }
5124
5125 NextInst = nullptr;
5126
5127 while (!Worklist.isEmpty()) {
5128 Instruction *I = Worklist.removeOne();
5129 if (!I)
5130 continue;
5131
5132 if (isInstructionTriviallyDead(I)) {
5133 eraseInstruction(*I);
5134 continue;
5135 }
5136
5137 MadeChange |= FoldInst(*I);
5138 }
5139
5140 return MadeChange;
5141}
5142
5143PreservedAnalyses VectorCombinePass::run(Function &F,
5144 FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM) {
5145 auto &AC = FAM.getResult<AssumptionAnalysis>(F);
5146 TargetTransformInfo &TTI = FAM.getResult<TargetIRAnalysis>(F);
5147 DominatorTree &DT = FAM.getResult<DominatorTreeAnalysis>(F);
5148 AAResults &AA = FAM.getResult<AAManager>(F);
5149 const DataLayout *DL = &F.getDataLayout();
5150 VectorCombine Combiner(F, TTI, DT, AA, AC, DL, TTI::TCK_RecipThroughput,
5151 TryEarlyFoldsOnly);
5152 if (!Combiner.run())
5153 return PreservedAnalyses::all();
5154 PreservedAnalyses PA;
5155 PA.preserveSet<CFGAnalyses>();
5156 return PA;
5157}
DL
